jobs.title: Managing Consultant –Authorization & Fraud SME, Advisors & Consulting Services
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Managing Consultant –Authorization & Fraud SME, Advisors & Consulting Services Managing Consultant – Authorization & Fraud SME Advisors & Consulting Services Services within Mastercard is responsible for acquiring, engaging, and retaining customers by managing fraud and risk, enhancing cybersecurity, and improving the digital payments experience. We provide value-added services and leverage expertise, data-driven insights, and execution. Our Advisors & Consulting Services team combines traditional management consulting with Mastercard’s rich data assets, proprietary platforms, and technologies to provide clients with powerful strategic insights and recommendations. Our teams work with a diverse global customer base across industries, from banking and payments to retail and restaurants. The Advisors & Consulting Services group has five specializations: Strategy & Transformation, Performance Analytics, Business Experimentation, Marketing, and Program Management. Our Strategy & Transformation consultants lead clients through impactful decision-making as they tackle strategic, tactical, operational, and transformational business challenges. They apply a broad set of problem-solving techniques to improve the client’s overall strategy, performance, and operations. Positions for different specializations and levels are available in separate job postings. Please review our consulting specializations to learn more about all opportunities and apply for the position that is best suited to your background and experience: https://careers.mastercard.com/us/en/consulting-specializations-at-mastercard Roles and Responsibilities Client Impact • Lead client engagements across a range of industries and problem statements, mainly by consulting on fraud management with a focus on related fraud products • Develop strategies and programs for large, regional, and global clients by leveraging data and technology solutions to unlock client value • Own key relationships with mid-level to senior client stakeholders and independently assess client agenda, internal culture, and change readiness Team Collaboration & Culture • Lead team to creative insights and sound business recommendations, and deliver impactful client presentations while growing team members’ roles and skills • Provide analytical and day-to-day project delivery team leadership, and create a collaborative and inclusive environment for all levels • Collaborate with internal Mastercard stakeholders including Product and Business Development to scope projects, create relevant solutions for clients, and build the firm's intellectual capital • Provide on-the-job training, coaching, and mentorship to junior consultants Qualifications Basic qualifications • Undergraduate degree with work experience in consulting, corporate strategy, business intelligence, business line management, or product management • Experience coaching and managing teams across multiple consulting engagements that involve structured problem solving and stakeholder management • Experience managing key client relationships • Knowledge of business KPIs, financials, and organizational leadership • Ability to identify new business development opportunities, and experience drafting proposals and scoping new opportunities • Logical, structured thinking, and affinity for numerical analysis • Advanced Word, Excel, and PowerPoint skills • Ability to manage multiple tasks and clients in a fast-paced, deadline-driven …

jobs.title: Vice President, Legal Compliance, EEMEA

Title and Summary Vice President, Legal Compliance, EEMEA Overview: The EEMEA Compliance Lead is responsible for leading, strengthening, and sustaining Mastercard’s Anti Corruption and regional compliance program across the Eastern Europe Middle East and Africa (EEMEA) Region. Reporting directly to the Senior Vice President, Anti Corruption and Regional Compliance, this role serves as the primary regional compliance leader and advisor to the business, supporting ethical growth while managing regulatory, reputational, and operational risk. The role provides strategic guidance and hands on oversight to regional and country leadership, sales, marketing, government sales, and other functions to ensure compliance with anti corruption laws, internal policies, and global standards. The position applies a risk based approach to embed compliance into business operations and decision making and works closely with Global Compliance, Legal, Risk, Finance, P&C, Global Affairs, Marketing, Internal Audit, and other stakeholders. The successful candidate will operate with a high degree of independence, judgment, and credibility, while remaining closely aligned with global headquarters and enterprise compliance priorities. Role: • Lead the EEMEA Anti Corruption compliance program, aligning regional execution with global strategy and applying a risk based approach to business activities. • Serve as the primary compliance advisor to EEMEA regional leadership, promoting a strong culture of ethics and compliance. Collaborate and lead, as needed, global Anti-Corruption projects. • Provide practical, timely compliance guidance on Anti Corruption, Code of Conduct, Conflicts of Interest, and related policies to support informed business decision making. • Provide advice on compliance risks associated with new markets, customers, products, acquisitions, joint ventures, and strategic initiatives, including contract review where required. • Oversee regional due diligence, approvals, and ongoing monitoring for high risk third parties, government related partners, resellers, suppliers, lobbyists, and external advisors. • Lead regional oversight of hospitality, sponsorships, charitable contributions, connected candidate reviews, and state owned entity engagements, including escalations and transparency requirements. • Develop and deliver risk based regional compliance training and targeted communications, including leadership led messaging and conduct office visits, when applicable, to engage with local leadership, document findings and follow up actions. • Participate in enterprise and regional risk assessments and compliance reviews. • Prepare and deliver regular compliance reporting to regional leadership and senior management. jobs.title: Senior Electrical Engineer
jobs.description: Bring your energy to Aggreko as Senior Electrical Engineer who will be provide specific engineering and technical support through the preparation of project specific designs and supporting technical documentations in Sales bids and Operational projects. Perform electrical fault level calculations and protection studies for high voltage systems Review of Cad based schematic and layout drawings with modification as required Preparation of Project Design Pack documentation in sales proposals and project contractual documentation Advise Sales team on specific design issues and propose engineering solutions Advise Operations team on the project design details during project mobilization process and on engineering issues during project installation and commissioning phases. Displays a commitment to Operational Excellence Initiatives Aggreko places a strong emphasis on Quality, Health, Safety and Environmental protection. Available to travel: On a national and international basis. Team player. Drive for results and Task completion. Resilience and follow-through. Fluent in English: A second language would be an advantage. Here’s what you’ll get: Full time position to be based in Dubai, UAE Competitive compensation package & annual bonus program Industry-leading benefit plans Continuous training and development with career growth opportunities Safety-focused culture Our people are can-do, positive, resilient and persistent. The Investment Banking Senior Analyst also serves as an intermediary in trading for clients. The overall objective of this role is to act as a strategic advisor to our clients by formulating investment strategies and raising capital for clients. jobs.title: Inspector - Civil (NDRC)
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Civil Inspector to join our team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. As a Civil Inspector, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the quality and compliance of our infrastructure and road projects. The Civil Inspector will be responsible for day-to-day supervision and inspection of stormwater drainage and microtunneling works. The role ensures all civil activities are executed in accordance with approved drawings, specifications, and project standards while maintaining compliance with quality, safety, and authority requirements. Review and apply all applicable codes, standards, and project specifications related to stormwater and microtunneling works (e.g., pipelines, shafts, chambers, culverts, and outfalls). Conduct daily inspections to verify that all construction activities are carried out in accordance with approved shop drawings, method statements, and specifications. Monitor and inspect excavation, shoring, backfilling, microtunneling drives, shaft construction, reinforcement, concrete pouring, waterproofing, and finishing of stormwater structures. Verify alignment, gradients, and levels for stormwater pipelines, chambers, and manholes to ensure compliance with design. Ensure the Contractor’s works comply with Dubai Municipality (DM), RTA, and relevant authority standards and approved quality control procedures. Record and report daily manpower, equipment, and material deliveries on-site. Supervise the execution of concrete curing, coating, and lining works for stormwater-related structures. Assist the Resident Engineer (RE) and Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE) in issuing site instructions and verifying the contractor’s responses and close-out. Coordinate with the contractor to resolve civil works issues and follow up on corrective actions. Prepare daily inspection reports, including observations, progress updates, test results, and safety notes. Support surveying and measurement verification for pipe routes, invert levels, and structural tolerances. Monitor and support the testing, commissioning, and handover of stormwater networks and associated civil works. Assist in reviewing RFIs, method statements, and variance requests related to civil activities. Coordinate with client representatives and authority inspectors during inspections and document all findings. Maintain organized records of inspections, NCRs, test results, as-built updates, and executed works. Bachelor's Degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering. Minimum 7 years of experience in construction and supervision of sewerage, stormwater, Irrigation and Miscellaneous works. with at least 3 years of experience in the UAE. Dubai Municipality experience is highly preferred. In-depth knowledge of existing NDRC, sewerage, drainage, stormwater, Irrigation networks in the emirates of Dubai.

jobs.title: Senior Architect
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: A Senior Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Minimum 15 years demonstrable experience in the all aspects of supervision and project administration of the Architectural works related to large-scale projects within Middle East in the consulting industry. Bachelor Degree in Architectural Engineering or equivalent. Membership of a recognized institution (i.e. PE/Peng, CEng Etc.) would be preferred. LEED GA or AP certifications would be a plus

jobs.title: Inspector - Structural (Roads, Bridges and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: AECOM is seeking an experienced Structural Inspector for a major Roads and Bridges project located in Dubai. The role involves both inspection and the supervision of Roads and Bridges works of the project to ensure that the activities are being executed in accordance with the project specification. Job Duties Be familiar with the project’s contract documents, detailed drawings, specifications, and health and safety measures together with any associated updates. Ensure the contractor/s compliance with the standards and specifications of the contract documents. Maintain records of the contractor human and equipment daily resources. Undertake all site supervision and inspections when required or instructed by the Resident Engineer (RE) or the Structural Engineer (SE). Liaise and daily follow up with the contractor. This is to ensure the contractor is managing its resources, health and safety, and equipment needs on the site. Prepare daily report to the RE or SE. Apply and implement all quality controls and quality procedures of the site.  Prepare any site measurements and surveying checks needed by the RE or SE for Bridge work & other associated structures. Co-ordinate and co-operate with client project manager during their site inspections in the presence of the RE or SE.  Monitor contractor QA/QC activities and maintain relevant records. Audit inspections for conformity to contract documents and contractor submitted plans. Minimum 7 years of experience within similar projects, with minimum 3 years in UAE. UAE experience is preferred Relevant experience in comparable structural & roads projects  Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering RTA Approval is preferred

jobs.title: Category Manager
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About The Team

Careem Grocery is on a mission to reinvent the way people shop for grocery, pharmacy and retail products. As an exciting new vertical within Careem (a startup within a startup), we’re in the early stages of building our product and have plenty of interesting and fundamental challenges to solve in order to achieve our ambitious growth goals in this burgeoning space.

About The Role

As a Category Manager, you’ll be responsible for driving our strategies on assortment by defining our selection by store and working with other teams on sourcing, pricing and promotions across our markets for categories. This is a unique role with the opportunity to create genuine impact for the lives of everyday people in our region. We’re looking for someone with solid experience in procuring and managing the entire supply chain of products to ensure we provide the best quality products at the right price.

What You'll Do

  • Build and lead the product line for Careem Groceries.
  • Design the go to market strategy.
  • Work with the team on maintaining the selection strategy in alignment with the overall department & business strategy and requirements.
  • Work with the team to identify the selection which need to be sourced within the categories.
  • Identify and sourcing key consumer trends in online grocery /Q-commerce business.
  • Source & negotiate the required selection with the best prices, quality and minimum MOQ that meets the business objectives, and ensure a competitive commercial terms with the suppliers.
  • Collaborate with the merchandising team to ensure the success of the launched campaigns.
  • Collaborate with the supply chain team to ensure the stock availability and smooth onboarding of the new added items.
  • Collaborate with the pricing team in sourcing competitive prices to maintain the category profitability.
  • Work closely with the supplier and maintain the relationship with them through business reviews and growth planning.
  • Work with the category assistant to ensure the hygiene of the item's content and maintain the right visibility on the App.


What You’ll Need

Successful candidates will have a passionate commitment to improving the lives of people, an insane focus on excellence and customer centricity, and a strong alignment with our core values and purpose.

  • At least 5+ years of experience in the grocery industry in Buying or Category Management . Time spent in tech or a start-up is a plus.
  • Experience negotiating with manufacturers, FMC …

jobs.title: Director, Product Management (Pricing & Assurance)
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Director, Product Management (Pricing & Assurance) Who is Mastercard? Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all. Overview Mastercard’s Transfer Solutions team in EEMEA is driving at scale innovation and growth to meet today’s demands for simple, certain, and secure international and domestic payments by individuals and businesses of all sizes. Mastercard Move is a portfolio of integrated assets and capabilities designed to service key verticals, customer segments and multiple payment flows. Through simple connectivity means, we enable partners the ability to reach a variety of endpoints—account, card, mobile wallet, cards and cash out locations —nearly anywhere in the world. The organization is looking for a Director, Product Management (Pricing & Assurance), Mastercard Move as part of the EEMEA Product Management Team for Cross Border Payments, to work with customers, who are primarily large Financial Institutions, Fin Techs, Bureau De Changes / Exchange Houses or Government Clearing Schemes to further the strategic and commercial agenda of Mastercard in the region focused on Cross Border Payments, which includes Low Value B2B / Commercial Payments, P2P Remittances and Purpose-based payments like Bill Payments, Tuition Payments, Utility, Social Security etc. The ideal candidate is aware of the Cross-Border Payments landscape and is aware of the intricate dynamics of how money moves between Institutions and Borders, being familiar with the role Correspondent and Bilateral Banking arrangements play in the setup, the evolving nature of SWIFT (and its evolution to ISO messaging standards), existing Settlement & Funding mediums, helping to identify the gaps and solutionize areas where Mastercard’s own and partner led solutions can add value. Role • This is a Pricing and Assurance role at its heart where you will work with Product Sales teams / Account Managers to ensure we are able to provide best commercial value to our customers alongside safeguarding long-term financial interests for the company. • Often, you would work with customers directly to help them formulate the right value story / financial model to ensure the product is able to provide the best Value Proposition to our clients and their customers. • Prepare / facilitate complex business cases within Mastercard at a customer / deal / portfolio level to ensure the right investments / financial support / prioritization is done for opportunities worth the merit. • Own the budgeting and forecasting cycle for the business, and partner with Finance colleagues upstream and Sales colleagues downstream to ensure we can project and hit the business numbers. • Formulate and track underlying business driver and enabler metrics on weekly / fortnightly / monthly level with cross-functional teams – including delivery timelines, growth rates etc. a …

jobs.title: Engineer - Civil
jobs.description: Supervise and monitor railway track construction activities in compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and project requirements. Coordinate track works including: Ballasted track Ballastless/slab track Rail installation Sleeper installation Turnouts and crossings Track alignment and geometry Review and verify shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals related to track works. Ensure track construction complies with railway standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. Monitor earthworks, formation preparation, and subgrade conditions before track installation. Coordinate with survey teams for track alignment, levels, and setting out verification. Supervise rail welding, fastening systems, and track fixing activities. Inspect ballast placement, compaction, and track stabilization works. Verify rail levels, gauge, cant, alignment, and track geometry tolerances. Coordinate interface works with: Civil structures Stations Utilities Signaling systems MEP systems Ensure compliance with quality assurance and quality control procedures. Review inspection requests and ensure timely closure of WIRs and inspection records. Raise NCRs and site observations for non-compliant works. Monitor contractor progress against approved baseline program and construction schedule. Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports. Coordinate with client, consultant, contractor, and railway authorities during execution. Ensure implementation of approved safety procedures and railway operational safety requirements. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track systems. Participate in track inspections, snagging, and handover activities. Review as-built drawings and final documentation before project handover. Monitor material delivery, storage, and handling of rails, sleepers, fasteners, and track components. Assist Resident Engineer and Project Manager in resolving technical and construction issues. Ensure compliance with international railway standards and project specifications. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from a recognized institution Minimum 8 years of professional experience in civil engineering, with a strong focus on rail/metro track works and infrastructure projects Proven experience in monitoring formation preparation, subgrade conditions, and readiness for track installation Demonstrated expertise in coordinating with survey teams for track alignment, levels, and setting-out verification Hands-on experience supervising rail welding, fastening systems, and track installation/fixing activities Strong knowledge of ballast placement, compaction techniques, and track stabilization processes In-depth understanding of track geometry requirements , including rail levels, gauge, cant, alignment, and tolerance control Experience in coordinating complex interface works across multiple disciplines , including: Civil structures Stations Utilities Signaling systems MEP systems Ability to interpret and apply technical drawings, specifications, and inspection/test plans (ITPs) Strong communication, coordination, and problem-solving skills within a multi-disciplinary rail project environment

jobs.description: The Senior Project Surveyor will lead post-contract commercial management activities on a major Abu Dhabi development. The role requires strong technical competence, sound commercial judgement, independent decision-making, and daily travel to site to support project delivery. The individual will supervise one Project Surveyor and work closely with, and report to, the Commercial & Contracts Lead. Cost & Budget Control: Act as custodian of the Cost Plan; update and report variances, risks, and forecasts. Track committed, incurred, and paid costs; prepare monthly cost and earned value reports. Pricing Schedules & BoQs : Prepare Pricing Schedules & BoQs for new works and changes. Support procurement processes, tender clarifications, and commercial evaluations. Contract Administration : Administer contracts in accordance with the Employer’s procedures and Construction Contract. Ensure compliance with contractual processes and approval limits. Payment Certification : Review payment applications for compliance, progress accuracy, and supporting documentation. Prepare payment certification and maintain payment logs. Change Management: Identify and track potential changes; prepare cost assessments and estimates. Maintain change registers and ensure compliance with contractual timeframes and prepare recommendations for Employer approval. Claims Management : Monitor compliance to reduce claim exposure. Review contractor claims for entitlement, accuracy, and contractual compliance. Prepare recommendation reports. Cost Management during Construction: Prepare monthly cost reports, cash-flow updates, and payment reconciliations. Evaluate variations, conduct cost comparisons, and verify payment applications. Team & Leadership: Supervise one Project Surveyor and oversee day-to-day commercial tasks. Provide direct support to the Commercial & Contracts Lead across all commercial and contractual matters. Additional Skills Required Strong English communication and reporting skills. Solid understanding of cost control, contract administration, change and claims procedures. Ability to negotiate, analyse data, and work independently. Proficiency in cost management software and MS Office. High professionalism, integrity, and organizational capability.     Must have a QS or Engineering degree or similar equivalent MRICS Desirable A minimum of 10 years’ experience on large-scale projects. Post-contract consultancy experience is essential. Must be able to travel daily to site within Abu Dhabi (vehicle and UAE driving licence required).

Excellent communication, reporting, and stakeholder management skills Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or a related technical discipline. jobs.title: Inspector - Civil (NDRC, Micro-tunneling)
jobs.description: The Civil Inspector will be responsible for day-to-day supervision and inspection of stormwater drainage and microtunneling works. The role ensures all civil activities are executed in accordance with approved drawings, specifications, and project standards while maintaining compliance with quality, safety, and authority requirements. Review and apply all applicable codes, standards, and project specifications related to stormwater and microtunneling works (e.g., pipelines, shafts, chambers, culverts, and outfalls). Conduct daily inspections to verify that all construction activities are carried out in accordance with approved shop drawings, method statements, and specifications. Monitor and inspect excavation, shoring, backfilling, microtunneling drives, shaft construction, reinforcement, concrete pouring, waterproofing, and finishing of stormwater structures. Verify alignment, gradients, and levels for stormwater pipelines, chambers, and manholes to ensure compliance with design. Ensure the Contractor’s works comply with Dubai Municipality (DM), RTA, and relevant authority standards and approved quality control procedures. Record and report daily manpower, equipment, and material deliveries on-site. Supervise the execution of concrete curing, coating, and lining works for stormwater-related structures. Assist the Resident Engineer (RE) and Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE) in issuing site instructions and verifying the contractor’s responses and close-out. Coordinate with the contractor to resolve civil works issues and follow up on corrective actions. Prepare daily inspection reports, including observations, progress updates, test results, and safety notes. Support surveying and measurement verification for pipe routes, invert levels, and structural tolerances. Monitor and support the testing, commissioning, and handover of stormwater networks and associated civil works. Assist in reviewing RFIs, method statements, and variance requests related to civil activities. Coordinate with client representatives and authority inspectors during inspections and document all findings. Maintain organized records of inspections, NCRs, test results, as-built updates, and executed works. **Required Qualifications:** Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent diploma Minimum 7 years of experience in civil inspection, including stormwater drainage and microtunneling projects Proven experience on Dubai Municipality (DM) and RTA projects Equivalency certification (as applicable by jurisdiction)

jobs.title: Manager, EEMEA Customer Success Enablement, Services

Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Manager, EEMEA Customer Success Enablement, Services Manager – EEMEA Customer Success Enablement, Services Overview Services within Mastercard is responsible for acquiring, engaging, and retaining customers by managing fraud and risk, enhancing cybersecurity, and improving the digital payments experience. We provide value-added services and leverage expertise, data-driven insights, and execution. Our Regional Customer Success teams play a crucial role in ensuring our customers achieve their desired outcomes while maximizing the value of our products and services. As advocates for customer experience, this team is accountable for a strong onboarding experience, providing ongoing strategic support, and fostering long-term relationships. They work collaboratively with cross-functional teams to address customer needs, gather feedback on product improvements, develop tailored strategies that enhance customer satisfaction and retention, build customer advocacy to support our B2B marketing campaigns and create leads for value expansion for our Business Development teams. By focusing on proactive engagement and personalized service with our customers, the team is dedicated to driving customer value and contributing to the overall growth of the organization. About the Role Are you passionate about building strong value propositions that empower customer-facing teams to deliver exceptional value? Join Mastercard’s Services Customer Success team as a Customer Success Operations & Enablement Manager. In this role, you’ll help shape the future of how we bring our network products to market grounded in value within the region. You’ll support the development of tools, processes, and insights that enable our teams to deliver consistent, high-impact customer experiences. The Customer Success Operations & Enablement Manager reports to the EEMEA Customer Success Lead and will support the development and delivery of strategic priorities for Customer Success within Mastercard Services. The primary focus of the role will be to support best-in-class go to market readiness for our CS teams within the region. You will be working with key partners such as Network Services, Product, Value Enablement, and Marketing in the region to enable common operating models and playbooks across CS teams, including developing flexible frameworks for key tools and customer segmentation strategies, creating a strong measurement framework of metrics and KPIs to evaluate performance and provide actionable insights to our colleagues. Responsibilities will include: CS Strategy & Launch Management • Collaborate with Product teams to identify what products should be in scope of Customer Success based on size of the business opportunity, Customer Personas, engagement model, skillset required…. • Participate in the Product launch process to decide if and how Customer Success should support, anticipating capacity and skillset needs, acting as the voice of the Customer for adequate product-market fit • Prepare the Product launch with Product Owners, ensuring we develop a consistent and customer-centric approach including documentation of personas and use cases, pitch decks that properly articulate the value proposition, demos, FAQs, objection handling, customer training… • Specifically for CSMs, prepare datasets per customer and document the engagement model, communication templates, value quantification tool, upsell and cross-sell motions. • Enable CSMs and  …

You will own the entire sales cycle, from opportunity identification to deal closure, ensuring alignment with national digital agendas and Cisco's portfolio. Your role is pivotal in building trusted C-suite relationships and orchestrating cross-functional teams to deliver innovative, integrated solutions that address critical public service challenges. Key Responsibilities • Strategic Account Management: Develop and execute comprehensive account plans to meet and exceed sales targets, expanding Cisco's footprint within the UAE public sector. • Customer Engagement: Build and maintain strong C-level relationships, acting as a trusted advisor aligned with government digital transformation goals and regulatory requirements. • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Lead and coordinate internal teams (engineering, services, partners) to deliver end-to-end solutions tailored to public sector needs. • Contract Negotiation: Manage complex contract negotiations and oversee the full sales cycle to ensure seamless delivery and customer satisfaction. • Market Intelligence: Analyze market trends, policy changes, and procurement processes to proactively position Cisco as the preferred technology partner. • Partner Ecosystem Development: Collaborate with partners to build a robust ecosystem that supports government procurement and project execution. • Sales Tools Utilization: Leverage Cisco's technology stack and AI-driven sales tools to enhance sales efficiency and pipeline accuracy. Minimum Qualifications • Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or a related Technical field. • Minimum 7 years of technology sales experience, with a focus on Public Sector or Government accounts in the UAE or Middle East. • Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas in the region. • Fluency in English (written and verbal) and proficiency in Arabic. • Deep understanding of UAE public sector procurement, regulatory environments, and government digital transformation initiatives. Preferred Qualifications • Master's degree or MBA. • Experience selling cloud, security, and software-defined networking solutions. • Established network of relationships within UAE government and semi-government organizations. • Strong presentation and storytelling skills, capable of simplifying complex technical concepts for diverse stakeholders. • Ability to work effectively in a matrixed organization and lead through influence. Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint. Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere. We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

jobs.title: Senior Production Engineer - Mature Assets
jobs.description: Senior Production Engineer Role Summary Provides senior-level assessment of well deliverability, operability, production risks, and optimization potential during asset screening. Ensures that individual well and field-level production profiles used for valuation are realistic, operable, and risk-adjusted. Key Responsibilities May perform nodal analysis using industry software such as PROSPER, PIPESIM, and others. Estimate achievable rates under realistic operating constraints when required. Identifying and document production-related risks for the asset risk register (water/gas breakthrough, sand, integrity, artificial lift, flow assurance). Should be able to sift through production and well history databases to pinpoint common issues seen in mature fields. Defining uptime assumptions, intervention frequency, and key OPEX drivers. Identifying debottlenecking, recompletion, lift optimization, and workover opportunities. Reviewing well files, completion histories, production data, and intervention records. Aligning production assumptions with reservoir forecasts and facilities capacities. Work closely with Reservoir Engineer on the assumptions and ensure risks are relayed to the Team Lead proficiently. Qualifications BSc in Petroleum or Mechanical Engineering. 15+ years production engineering experience across operated and non-operated assets. Deep expertise in nodal analysis, artificial lift, well interventions, and flow assurance. Strong ability to translate operational realities into screening-level assumptions. jobs.title: Senior Reservoir Engineer - Mature Assets
jobs.description: Senior Reservoir Engineer Role Summary Provides senior reservoir engineering input to asset screening, focusing on recoverable volumes, production forecasts, recovery mechanisms, and reservoir-related risks. Supports the Team Lead by translating subsurface uncertainty into recovery and development implications suitable for economic evaluation. Key Responsibilities Estimating STOIIP/GIIP and recoverable volumes using volumetrics, analogs, material balance, and fit-for-purpose models. Defining reservoir-related risks and uncertainties for inclusion in the asset risk register (connectivity, drive mechanism, sweep, injectivity, pressure support). Developing screening-level production profiles (P90/P50/P10) consistent with reservoir behavior and facilities constraints. Screening well count, spacing, depletion strategy, and EOR potential at a concept level and be able to translate that into impacted service lines within Baker Hughes, as well as potential partners. Review existing development plans and challenge status quo or define new tested concepts from analogue fields. Reviewing reservoir models, test data, forecasts, and reserves statements during acquisitions or farm-ins. Working closely with G&G and Production Engineering to ensure coherent subsurface and well performance assumptions. Qualifications BSc in Petroleum/Reservoir Engineering (MSc preferred). 15+ years reservoir engineering experience including asset evaluation and due diligence. Strong expertise in DCA, material balance, reserves/resources classification, and uncertainty analysis. Commercially aware, with experience supporting investment decisions. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Senior Principal, Geoscience & Petroleum, Disciplinary Engineering and Science

jobs.description: AECOM are seeking a highly experienced Associate Director - Cost Management,  to join our masterplanning and infrastructure cost team. jobs.title: Tracks Inspector
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Civil Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive inspections of railway tracks and rail infrastructure at our freight facilities, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational excellence. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures, including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

jobs.title: Inspector - Utilities
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Civil Inspector - Utilities to join our team in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will conduct comprehensive inspections of utility infrastructure and systems to ensure compliance with local regulations and industry standards. You will demonstrate exceptional organizational skills, technical expertise, and a commitment to maintaining the highest safety and quality standards across utility operations. While the role focuses on civil works for wet utilities (water, drainage, sewer), you will coordinate with dry utilities stakeholders where applicable. Key Responsibilities Conduct routine, scheduled, and complaint-based inspections of utility infrastructure, with primary emphasis on water distribution, stormwater/drainage, and sewerage networks; coordinate with electrical, gas, and telecommunications stakeholders as applicable to interface points. Document inspection findings in detail and prepare comprehensive, accurate inspection reports using established protocols and software systems. Identify code violations, safety hazards, maintenance deficiencies, and areas requiring immediate attention or remediation. Verify compliance with local UAE regulations, industry standards, and established safety protocols during all inspections. Coordinate with maintenance, repair, and operations teams to communicate findings and recommend corrective actions. Maintain organized and up-to-date inspection records, databases, and documentation for audit and reference purposes. Respond promptly to stakeholder complaints and service requests by conducting follow-up inspections and investigations. Provide technical recommendations for system repairs, upgrades, or replacements based on inspection findings. Collaborate with regulatory agencies and stakeholders to ensure adherence to utility service standards. Participate in training programs to stay current with evolving regulations and inspection best practices. Plan and execute inspections in operational environments with restricted access, aligning activities with site safety, permit-to-work requirements, and operational windows typically associated with logistics or freight-adjacent corridors. Handle sensitive information discreetly and maintain confidentiality regarding project scope, operations, and locations. Minimum 5 years of professional experience in utilities inspection, infrastructure assessment, or related field Strong technical knowledge of utility systems including water, electrical, gas, and telecommunications infrastructure Experience working on a freight facility / Rail Experience

jobs.title: Senior Technician - Digital Delivery
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Senior Technician - Digital Delivery to join our organization in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for managing and optimizing our digital delivery infrastructure, ensuring seamless technical operations, and providing expert-level support to maintain system performance and reliability. You will work collaboratively with cross-functional teams to implement solutions, resolve complex technical issues, and drive continuous improvement in our digital service delivery. .Roles & Responsibilities Develop electrical dry utilities designs including power distribution, street lighting, telecom, and ELV systems in accordance with local authority standards (e.g., DEWA, Etisalat, etc.). Prepare design drawings, calculations, load schedules, and technical reports. Ensure designs comply with codes, regulations, and project specifications. Perform design coordination with civil, architectural, and other MEP disciplines. Create and maintain accurate 3D BIM models for electrical dry utilities using software such as Revit, Civil 3D, or similar. Ensure BIM models meet LOD requirements and BIM execution plans (BEP). Conduct clash detection and resolve coordination issues using tools like Navisworks. Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure integrated project delivery. Maintain proper documentation and version control of BIM models and design files. Ensure all submissions meet authority guidelines and requirements. Address authority review comments and update designs accordingly. Ensure adherence to company BIM standards, CAD standards, and QA/QC procedures. Conduct internal reviews of models and drawings for accuracy and completeness. Maintain consistency across all project deliverables. Participate in project meetings, coordination workshops, and technical discussions. Provide technical support during construction and site coordination. Assist in resolving site issues and design discrepancies. Support the improvement and streamlining of design workflows and BIM processes. Contribute to the development of BIM standards, templates, and libraries. Stay updated with latest BIM technologies and engineering practices.   5+ years of experience in technical support, IT operations, or digital infrastructure management Proven expertise in digital delivery platforms and technologies Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues Proficiency with IT service management (ITSM) tools and ticketing systems Solid understanding of network architecture, cloud platforms, and system infrastructure Experience with automation tools and scripting for operational efficiency Excellent organizational and time management skills Strong technical documentation and communication abilities Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment Experience with vendor management and service level agreement (SLA) monitoring Preferred: Advanced technical certifications (CompTIA, Cisco, AWS, or equivalent) Preferred: Experience with infrastructure-as-code (IaC) tools and DevOps practices Preferred: Leadership or mentoring experience with technical teams

jobs.title: BIM Coordinator (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: Job Summary The Senior Technician - Digital Delivery  will be responsible for managing and coordinating Building Information Modeling (BIM) deliverables for large-scale infrastructure and bridge projects, including utilities, landscape works, and structural components. The role involves overseeing BIM outputs from multiple project parties, ensuring cross-discipline model coordination, and maintaining compliance with project BIM standards and the BIM Execution Plan (BEP). The BIM Lead / Coordinator will act as the central point of contact for BIM-related matters between the supervision team, design teams, and external stakeholders. Key Responsibilities Coordination of BIM Deliverables Oversee BIM deliverables Ensure effective model coordination between all project disciplines. Organize and lead regular BIM coordination meetings and technical workshops. Coordinate with external project participants to manage tie-ins and interface points. Model Review & Compliance Review submitted models for compliance with the BEP, project standards, and Level of Development (LOD) requirements. Perform quality checks on geometry, metadata, and model structure. Facilitate clash detection and resolution using Navisworks or equivalent software. Information Management Ensure proper use of data exchange protocols and the Common Data Environment (CDE). Track model submissions, revisions, and approval workflows. Support document control teams in organizing model-related deliverables and ensuring traceability. Reporting Prepare and issue weekly or milestone-based BIM coordination reports summarizing progress, identified issues, and potential risks. Update the supervision management team on BIM coordination status and outstanding actions. Standards & Compliance Ensure all modeling works align with the client’s BIM standards and project-specific requirements. Enforce adherence to established naming conventions, classification systems, and data structures. Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Minimum 7 years of experience in BIM coordination, preferably on infrastructure and bridge projects. Proficient in Autodesk Civil 3D, Revit, Navisworks, and ACC/BIM 360. Strong understanding of multi-discipline coordination, particularly for civil, structural, and landscape works. Excellent communication, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.

jobs.title: Senior Supply Chain Manager
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About the team

Careem Grocery is reinventing how people shop for groceries, pharmacy, and everyday essentials, on-demand, in minutes. We're a startup within a startup: a small, high-conviction team operating at speed, with real ownership over outcomes. We're building the supply chain infrastructure that makes that promise possible, and we're looking for someone to own the demand side of it.

About the role

This is a senior individual contributor and cross-functional leadership role for someone who is equally comfortable writing a SQL query, challenging a commercial team's promo forecast, and presenting supply risk to a VP. You'll own demand planning end-to-end; from statistical modelling to S&OP facilitation and you'll be expected to build and improve the systems, not just run them.

What you'll do

  • Forecasting & Modelling - Build and maintain demand forecasting models across SKUs, categories, and dark store locations. Go beyond Excel — leverage statistical methods (time series, regression, ML-assisted forecasting) and own the tooling decisions that get us there. You should be comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and sense-checking model outputs against ground truth.
  • Demand Variability & Scenario Planning - Develop frameworks to manage volatility from seasonality, promotions, new product launches, and macroeconomic signals. Run scenario models before they become supply crises.
  • S&OP Process Leadership - Own the weekly and monthly S&OP cadence. Facilitate cross-functional alignment across commercial, ops, and logistics. Surface gaps early, propose solutions, and drive decisions, not just document them.
  • Inventory Optimisation - Set and monitor inventory targets by SKU and location to balance availability against holding cost and waste. Work closely with category and logistics teams to design replenishment logic that performs at quick commerce speed.
  • Data & Tooling- You'll be hands-on with data. Write and maintain SQL queries for demand signals, build dashboards that non-technical stakeholders can act on, and explore AI/ML tooling to improve forecast accuracy. If a process can be automated, you'll be the one to build the case and execute it.
  • Insight & Communication - Translate complex demand signals into clear narratives for senior leadership. Forecast updates, variance analysis, risk flags — you own the story and the numbers behind it.

What you need

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a closely re …

jobs.title: Vice President - AML Complex Investigations Analyst (UAE Nationals Only)
jobs.description: The Compl AML Intel Officer is a strategic professional who stays abreast of developments within own field and contributes to directional strategy by considering their application in own job and the business. Recognized technical authority for an area within the business. Requires basic commercial awareness. There are typically multiple people within the business that provide the same level of subject matter expertise. Developed communication and diplomacy skills are required in order to guide, influence and convince others, in particular colleagues in other areas and occasional external customers. Significant impact on the area through complex deliverables. Provides advice and counsel related to the technology or operations of the business. Work impacts an entire area, which eventually affects the overall performance and effectiveness of the sub-function/job family. Responsibilities: Analyze and proactively identify risks and new AML concerns. Review recent news, court documents, and public/private sector white papers. Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets; review computer reports, printouts, and performance indicators to locate and correct code problems. Gather raw data from prior AML Investigations, subpoenas, and prior escalations to detect trends and “connect the dots” amongst disparate data sets. Interpret data, analyze results using statistical techniques and provide ongoing reports; present data in charts, graphs, tables, design and develop relational databases for collecting data and building or designing data input/data collection screens. Identify and research high risk areas / typologies and trigger events for risk severity and sensitivity evaluation. Work collaboratively to analyze and review typologies to establish review processes. Utilize pertinent information to write clear and concise summary on the findings of the investigation to advise senior management on next steps. Make recommendations to senior management on next course of action – either relationship retention or termination and, when warranted, prepare Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs). Additional duties as assigned. Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency. Qualifications: AML Certification 6-10 years prior work experience in a financial institution with job specific work experience in AML Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills Education: Bachelor’s/University degree, Master’s degree preferred This job description provides a high-level review of the types of work performed. Other job-related duties may be assigned as required. ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family Group: Compliance ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family: AML Intelligence ------------------------------------------------------ Time Type: Full time ------------------------------------------------------ Most Relevant Skills Business Acumen, Credible Challenge, Data Analysis, Laws and Regulations, Management Reporting, Policy and Procedure, Referral and Escalation, Risk Controls and Monitors, Risk Identification and Assessment, Risk Remediation. ------------------------------------------------------ Other Relevant Skills For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. ------------------------------------------------------ Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, stat …

jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: AECOM's Construction Supervision practice is currently seeking an experienced Structures Manager, to join one of our projects, based in Abu Dhabi. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in structural engineering and construction management, with a strong ability to lead and coordinate complex projects. Responsibilities: Project Management: Lead and manage structural aspects of construction supervision projects, ensuring they are completed on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards. Design Coordination: Collaborate with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to ensure structural designs are integrated seamlessly into overall project plans. Construction Supervision: Oversee the day-to-day operations of construction sites, ensuring adherence to safety protocols and structural standards. Client Liaison: Act as the primary point of contact for clients regarding structural matters, providing regular updates and addressing any concerns or issues that arise. Team Leadership: Manage and mentor a team of structural engineers and construction professionals, fostering a collaborative and productive work environment. Quality Control: Implement and maintain quality control procedures to ensure all structural work meets the required standards and specifications. Documentation: Prepare and review project documentation, including structural reports, drawings, and specifications. Problem Solving: Identify and resolve any structural issues or challenges that arise during the construction process.   Bachelor's degree in Structural Engineering, Civil Engineering, or a related field. Master's degree preferred. Minimum 20 years of experience in structural engineering and construction management, with at least 5 years in a managerial role leading the construction supervision of residential or retail projects.

jobs.title: Document Control Specialist
jobs.description: Responsible for the administration of a comprehensive document control system to support program-wide administrative, engineering, and operational activities. This role ensures timely, accurate, and systematic management of all project documentation in alignment with project and client requirements, with a particular focus on electronic document control through platforms such as Aconex. Maintain and control all technical documentation including specifications, IFC and shop drawings, vendor documents, calculation sheets, and revision instructions, both electronically and physically. Ensure proper use and upkeep of the Electronic Document Management System (EDMS), particularly Aconex, for receiving, tracking, and distributing project documentation. Prepare and maintain accurate logs and registers such as Critical / Non-Critical Documents, GFC Drawings, Shop Drawings, RFIs, NCRs, CARs, and others as required for monthly progress reports. Issue documents using approved transmittal formats in accordance with project procedures to the client, consultants, site teams, and company stakeholders. Monitor incoming and outgoing correspondences, ensuring timely routing, response tracking, and archiving. Coordinate with Head Office and Vendors to obtain updated versions of all documents and ensure the latest revisions are distributed to relevant parties. Support the compilation and submission of final as-built documentation in compliance with approved document closeout procedures. Assist in the preparation of daily, weekly, and monthly reports by maintaining regular records of site document activity. Maintain the Master Document Register for each project and ensure readiness for progress reporting and milestone payment submissions. Track license registration and renewal requirements as per applicable project regulations. Minimum Qualifications: Degree preferred but not essential; relevant certifications in document control are an advantage. Experience: Minimum 7 years of experience in a similar role, preferably within a construction supervision or engineering design consultancy. Proven experience with Aconex on large-scale infrastructure, building, or industrial projects is essential.

jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. Title and Summary Vice President, Strategy Pricing & Interchange You will be leading the economics strategy for Mastercard products and services for Eastern Europe Middle East and Africa Region (EEMEA). You will design, assess and implement pricing and interchange driven initiatives to strengthen Mastercard’s’ competitive position on product and country level. By enhancing and shaping the evolution of our economics you will play an important role in the development and adoption of innovative payment solutions. You will have broad exposure within the organization and work closely with Country, Division, and Functional management teams and liaise with partners in the Legal, Finance and Business Development teams. You will serve as strategic thought partner for senior management and contribute to key projects for Mastercard’s strategy. The role reports into Business Excellence Lead for EEMEA. Key Responsibilities: • Deliver holistic assessments on pricing and interchange initiatives along strategic, customer, financial impact, risk and feasibility angles. (e.g., run financial impact simulations on country, product and customer level or run competitive economics analysis) • Supervise cost studies that underpin the Interchange propositions (including members briefings and meetings) • Prepare recommendations on innovative new product business cases and necessary materials for decision making and present recommendations to Senior Management • Collaborate closely with regional and country management across EEMEA • Participating in cross-functional teams and liaising closely with various internal and external stakeholders, e.g., Product, Finance, Legal , Customers, consultants, etc. • Identifying and designing opportunities to support EEMEA objectives Key Skills & Experience: • Proven demonstration of top class problem solving skills • Excellent analytical skills: structuring and conducting rigorous analysis • Ability to think strategically and develop a strategic view on Mastercard’s services and economics- understanding the financial fundamentals and architecture behind Mastercard business • Excellent communication skills; able to liaise and work with a customer oriented mindset towards its internal and external counterparts (e.g., accountability, sense of urgency, excellence). • Strong drive and curiosity to learn, creativity, passion to achieve and excel • Ability to manage projects and use project-management skills for managing complex processes • Strong team player who can operate effectively in a complex matrix • Strong background in finance and analytics • Excellent knowledge of Agentic Automation, Excel, and other Information Management tools • Fluent in English Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; jobs.title: Senior Site Architect

jobs.description: A Senior Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. jobs.title: Team Lead Sales - Dubai
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About the Team
You will be part of a dynamic team composed of Careem experienced professionals to build and grow Careem Food to become the market leader. We strive to embody our values of ownership, shooting to the moon, and being of service to customers, partners, colleagues, and captains. As Sales Team Lead, you will manage the team responsible for executing our sales strategy and vision by successfully signing and onboarding new partners and improving the customer experience through increased restaurant supply.

About the Role
As the Sales Team Lead for Careem, you will be responsible for driving new restaurant partnerships across UAE, with a strong focus on Dubai. Your leadership will be instrumental in motivating and guiding our sales team to achieve ambitious growth targets. Your expertise in sales strategy, team management, and market insights will play a pivotal role in shaping our success across this key market.

What you'll do

Leadership and Team Management:

  • Lead, inspire, and manage a team of 8 sales professionals to achieve sales targets.
  • Provide coaching, training, and support for the team’s professional growth.
  • Support the team in closing deals and lead key negotiations where necessary.
  • Drive pipeline management and improve efficiency.

Sales Strategy and Planning:

  • Collaborate with the Head of Sales to develop and execute winning sales strategies for Dubai.
  • Identify and prioritize must-have brands and high-demand restaurant categories to close the competitive inventory gap.
  • Use creative, data-driven approaches to craft pitches and materials to close deals.
  • Implement and refine processes to enhance team performance.
  • Work cross-functionally to ensure successful onboarding and sustained partner growth.
  • Conduct regular competitive benchmarking to identify supply gaps versus key competitors.
  • Build and maintain a target list of priority brands and work relentlessly to bring them onto Careem Food.
  • Partner with internal teams (marketing, ops, account management) to ensure newly signed brands are set up for success and retained.

What you'll need

  • 3–5 years of experience managing sales teams with a proven track record in direct sales.
  • Experience transforming teams through strong strategy and management practices.
  • Demonstrated ability to build a high-performance culture and a unified team vision.
  • Proficiency in data analysis and tools such as Excel …

jobs.title: Reciprocating Compressors - Technical Sales Senior Advisor - New Units
jobs.description: Do you want to be part of a global team transforming the energy industry? Do you have experience in Reciprocating Compressors? Join our team! Baker Hughes stands as a leading global energy‑technology company, delivering innovative solutions that make energy safer, cleaner, and more efficient across the entire value chain. Securing solid, continuous, and easy exchange of information with the engineering/product management/manufacturing center of excellence and senior management to promote valuable solutions and quick decisions for the customers. Leading the technical qualification of New Products with customers and support commercialization strategy. jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Screen and assess candidates Review applications, conduct initial screenings, and evaluate candidates against role requirements, ensuring a consistent and high-quality shortlisting process. jobs.title: Engineer - Utilities
jobs.description: Supervise and inspect the construction of dry utility networks including HV/LV cables, street lighting, telecom (Etisalat/Du), ITS, and other related infrastructure to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and authority standards (RTA, DEWA, Etisalat, Du, and DM). Review excavation, duct laying, backfilling, and cable pulling works to verify compliance with project specifications and approved method statements. Monitor and verify jointing, termination, and chamber installation for electrical and telecom networks in accordance with approved standards. Ensure proper coordination and interface between dry and wet utility networks to avoid clashes and ensure smooth implementation. Inspect trench preparation, bedding, and protection layers before and after utility installation. Review and comment on shop drawings, material submittals, method statements, and as-built drawings related to dry utilities. Monitor contractor’s work to ensure compliance with safety requirements, quality control procedures, and approved ITPs (Inspection and Test Plans). Witness and verify inspection requests (WIRs) and ensure all tests such as continuity, insulation resistance, and functional testing are properly conducted and documented. Coordinate with local authorities (RTA, DEWA, Etisalat, Du, Civil Defense, and DM) for inspections, NOCs, and approvals. Participate in progress and coordination meetings to discuss technical issues, utility conflicts, and schedule updates. Verify trench reinstatement and surface finishing works are carried out as per project and authority requirements. Ensure the contractor provides adequate protection and marking of utilities after installation. Support the Resident Engineer and Utility Engineer in resolving site issues and verifying as-built information before handover. Ensure all testing, commissioning, and handover documentation for dry utilities are complete and in compliance with project closeout requirements Bachelor's degree in civil engineering or related field Minimum of 7-12 years of experience in Utilities engineering. Masters is prefrable but not mandatory
