jobs.title: Civil Inspector - (rails/ tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Civil Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive inspections of railway tracks and rail infrastructure at our freight facilities, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational excellence. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures, including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

jobs.title: Director, Strategy and M&A
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Director, Strategy and M&A Mastercard is seeking a Director, Strategy, based in Dubai, UAE. This role is pivotal in shaping and driving Mastercard's strategic growth initiatives across Eastern Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EEMEA). This role will lead identifying strategic growth opportunities, aligned with Mastercard’s strategic priorities in the EEMEA region. Key Responsibilities • Lead the development and execution of the strategic plan for EEMEA, directly shaping regional, corporate strategy and priorities • Oversee and drive high-priority strategic projects, structuring complex deals, conducting rigorous analyses, and delivering actionable insights and recommendations to the senior leadership team • Lead the regional localization agenda and drive the strategy to expand share in local payment flows • Monitor industry trends and competitive landscape, proactively sharing insightful analyses and reports with the broader team and senior executives • Lead strategic and financial analyses to identify and evaluate growth opportunities, market trends, competitive positioning, and operational performance • Engage and build relationships at the executive level both internally and externally to influence and drive strategic outcomes All About You • Extensive combined experience in Strategy within the payments and financial services sectors is a must • Deep understanding and experience within payments and financial services and related industries is a must • Proven track record of successfully leading large-scale, complex, cross-functional strategic projects in global, matrixed organizations • Demonstrated capability in strategic planning, advanced analytics, financial modeling, transaction valuation, and risk assessment, hands-on proficiency with analytics platforms • Exceptional executive-level communication and presentation skills, adept at influencing diverse stakeholder groups and driving strategic alignment • Strong leadership skills with experience building, motivating, and managing high-performing teams in a dynamic, matrixed organization • Experience of managing multiple stakeholders in a multicultural environment, across multiple geographic locations. Strong negotiating and influencing skills, with the ability to manage delivery through others who do not report directly. • Ability to work collaboratively and both independently and in a team environment. • Excellent writing and presentation skills, extensive experience in communicating in international environment at senior management level • 7+ years of progressive business experience and demonstrated results in strategic, financial and/or commercially focused roles. Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

jobs.title: ARMO Mechanic (TORQUE TURNS)
jobs.location: AE-ABU DHABI-AL GHAITH YARD, MUSSAFAH INDUSTRIAL 16th STREET
jobs.description: Do you enjoy working with equipment? Do you relish the prospect of working with cutting-edge energy products? Join our Team! At Baker Hughes our purpose is clear. We take energy forward making it safer, cleaner and more efficient for people and the planet. Partner with the best The Torque Turns Computer Maintenance / SALVO LAB Technician is responsible for repairing, installing, maintaining, calibrating, and troubleshooting torque-turn monitoring systems used during tubular running operations to ensure reliable oil and Gas well Construction. This position requires candidates to demonstrate well-established technical knowledge , focused on the calibration, operation, verification, and analysis of Torque & Turns monitoring systems used during oil and gas well Construction/completion operations . As a Maintenance Technician - Torque Turns, you will be responsible for: Installing, configuring, and maintaining torque-turn computer systems, data acquisition units, Torque subs and associated sensors (e.g., load cells, encoders, proximity sensors). Performing equipment calibration, validation, and function testing to ensure measurement accuracy and compliance with company/client requirements. Monitoring live torque-turn plots during casing, tubing, and completion operations; providing immediate feedback to the operating team when trends deviate from specifications. Diagnosing and resolving hardware and software faults under field conditions, escalating complex issues when required. Executing preventive maintenance, pre-job checks, and routine system inspections to maximize uptime and reliability. Reviewing and analyzing torque-turn data to identify anomalies and connection make-up issues. Providing technical support to field crews during rig-up/rig-down and operational activities. Maintaining an inventory of spares and consumables; confirming readiness of contingency equipment and backup systems. Complying with HSE policies, procedures, and permit-to-work requirements; participating in toolbox talks and risk assessments. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will have: Technical Diploma or Bachelor’s /Higher Educational Degree in Electronics, Instrumentation, Electrical, Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Electromechanics, Computer or Instrumentation Technology or a related industrial and Oil & Gas discipline. 3–5 years of experience in oil & gas field operations (torque-turn or tubular running services). Solid understanding of torque-turn principles, connection make-up theory, and acceptance criteria. Hands-on experience with torque-turn software platforms and data acquisition hardware. Working knowledge of Power tongs, Power Units (Electric & Diesel) casing running tools (CRT), and tubular handling equipment. Ability to interpret torque-turn graphs, connection specifications, and operating procedures. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: · Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities · Comprehensive private medical ca …

Design Coordination: Collaborate with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to ensure design integrity and compliance with project specifications. jobs.title: Manager - Electrical
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: We are hiring an experienced Electrical Manager to lead all electrical supervision activities on a construction project, ensuring technical compliance, safety, quality, and timely delivery. The role will manage a team of electrical engineers/inspectors, interface with the client, contractor, designers, and authorities, and drive resolution of technical issues from pre-construction through testing, commissioning, and handover. Key Responsibilities Technical Leadership & Design Assurance • Lead technical reviews of shop drawings, material submittals, method statements, ITPs, and as-builts for compliance with contract, IFC drawings, and applicable codes/standards (e.g., IEC, IEEE, NEC, BS, local codes). • Provide authoritative responses to RFIs/technical queries and maintain a robust decision log. • Verify electrical calculations (load, short-circuit, protection coordination, volt drop, earthing/grounding, lighting, cable sizing) and propose optimizations where appropriate. Construction Supervision & Quality Control • Plan, assign, and oversee daily inspections (WIR/MIR), surveillance, and hold/witness points; manage NCRs, CARs, and closeouts. • Monitor installation of LV/MV/HV distribution, substations/switchgear, transformers, generators, UPS, earthing and lightning protection, lighting, small power, and ELV systems (fire alarm, PA/VA, BMS, CCTV, access control, structured cabling). • Ensure proper storage, handling, and installation per manufacturer requirements and project specifications. • Validate temporary electrical works and energization plans for safe construction staging. Testing, Commissioning & Handover • Lead FAT/SAT, pre-commissioning, commissioning, and integrated systems testing; approve procedures and verify test records. • Coordinate protection settings, relay testing, SCADA/BMS integration, and utility interfaces up to energization. • Oversee readiness for handover: snag/punch lists, O&M manuals, asset registers, training, spares, warranties, and final as-builts. Interface, Stakeholder & Authority Coordination • Coordinate with civil/MEP disciplines to resolve clashes and sequence works; drive BIM-based coordination when applicable. • Liaise with client representatives, designers, utilities, and authorities to secure NOCs, permits, inspections, and energization approvals. • Chair electrical progress/coordination meetings; issue clear action plans and minutes. Schedule, Cost & Contract Administration • Track electrical progress vs. baseline; review look-aheads and recovery plans; validate measurement and earned value for electrical scope. • Support change management: evaluate contractor claims/variations for technical merit and impact; produce recommendations. • Administer contract requirements (e.g., FIDIC or equivalent): deliverables, notices, approvals, and close-out. Minimum Qualifications • Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering (BSc/BEng) from an accredited institution. • 15+ years’ experience in electrical engineering with at least 5 years in construction supervision consultancy and 2–3 years in a lead/management role. • Proven delivery on medium to large-scale building, infrastructure, or industrial projects. • Strong knowledge of relevant codes/standards (IEC/IEEE/NEC/BS) and local authority requirements. • Demonstrated experience with testing, commissioning, and utility/authority energization processes. • Excellent English communication (written and verbal); strong report writing and stakeholder management. Preferred Qualifications & Certifications • Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or related discipline. • Professional registration (e.g., CEng, PE) and/or PMP. • Chartered membership or active affiliation with IEEE/IES/IEI (or equivalent). • Prior experience with FIDIC-based contracts. • Regional authority experience (e.g., utilities/municipal approvals) for the project location.

jobs.title: Senior Engineer - Track & Alignment
jobs.description: General: • Performs specific and moderate portions of a broader assignment of an experienced engineer. • Gathers and correlates basic engineering data using established and well-defined procedures. • Works on detailed or routine engineering assignments involving calculations and relatively simple tests. • Proposes approach to solve new problems encountered using modifications of standard procedures or methods developed in previous assignments. • Identifies discrepancies in results. • Provides guidance to entry level engineers. • Performs work in accordance with agreed upon budget and schedule with little supervision. • Independently performs all the tasks necessary to complete primary design elements for engineering works.  • Performance at this level requires developmental experience in a professional position.   Role Specific:   Responsible for the design, development, and coordination of railway trackwork systems, including plain line track, switches and crossings (S&C), crossovers, depots, and special trackwork. Produces and reviews track layouts, track geometry, turnout arrangements, rail and sleeper specifications, and trackform solutions to ensure compliance with project requirements, operational needs, and applicable standards. Coordinates closely with civil, systems, signalling, and rolling stock disciplines to resolve interfaces and optimize track design. Supports design assurance, technical reviews, constructability assessments, and the development of technical reports and specifications. Provides technical guidance to junior engineers and contributes to the successful delivery of metro, freight, and mainline rail projects through all project phases from concept to detailed design, including construction phases.   Experience:   10+ years rail experience including GCC region       Bachelor of Science or Engineering from an accredited University in one or more of the following subjects and a Master of Science / Engineering or PhD would be preferred. CEng, PE, or equivalent licensing/registration as a professional engineer is advantageous. Relevant registration required in Qatar, Bahrain and UAE.

jobs.title: Principal Engineer - Track & Alignment
jobs.description: General:   • Senior technical resource may serve as technical advisor for team • Provides specialized technical input to studies and design for staff's specific area of expertise. • Develops study and design procedures to facilitate high quality cost effective work by others. • Participates in interdisciplinary review of project deliverables. • Develops construction cost estimates and estimates of technical efforts for projects. • Uses expertise in all steps of completing discipline component of PS&E package. • Performs quality control review of design calculations or drawings. • Prepares technical specification sections. • Provides input to the development of engineering budget and schedule to meet requirements.   Role Specific:   Provides technical leadership for the planning, design, and delivery of railway track alignment solutions across metro, freight, high-speed, and conventional rail projects. Leads the development and review of horizontal and vertical alignments, track geometry, corridor optimization, and operational integration to ensure safe, efficient, and compliant railway infrastructure. Acts as the technical authority for alignment design, managing complex engineering challenges, design assurance activities, and multidisciplinary interfaces. Responsible for overseeing alignment teams, establishing design methodologies, reviewing key deliverables, and ensuring compliance with applicable standards and client requirements. Supports project management through technical risk management, stakeholder engagement, bid preparation, and strategic decision-making. Mentors and develops engineers while driving innovation, quality, and best practice throughout all project phases from feasibility and concept development through to detailed design and construction support.   Experience:   15+ years experience including GCC region       Bachelor of Science or Engineering from an accredited University in one or more of the following subjects and a Master of Science / Engineering or PhD would be preferred. CEng, PE, or equivalent licensing/registration as a professional engineer is advantageous.   Relevant registration required in Qatar, Bahrain and UAE.

jobs.title: Senior Planning/Project Controls Engineer
jobs.description: We are seeking a seasoned Project Planning/Project Controls Engineer to lead schedule and cost controls across complex infrastructure and buildings programs.You will shape robust baselines, drive project performance, and provide clear insights that help our clients make confident decisions. Ensure project schedules comply with RTA specifications, guidelines, and standard work breakdown structures (WBS) for roads, bridges, and infrastructure projects. Coordinate planning activities in line with RTA approval procedures, including interim milestones, traffic staging, and sectional handover requirements. Monitor and integrate RTA authority approvals (NOCs, permits, traffic diversion approvals, utility coordination) into the project schedule. Review and assess contractors’ programs against RTA contractual milestones, key performance indicators (KPIs), and phasing requirements. Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports. Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Quantity Surveying, or related field (Master’s preferred). Senior planning/controls role requiring 15+ years’ experience, strong base cost and standard planning capability, consultancy and local market experience, and a proven track record across infrastructure and buildings. Advanced proficiency in Primavera P6, cost control, EVM, risk analysis, and executive‑quality reporting is essential. Client‑facing leadership, clear communication, and rigorous governance will be central to success.

jobs.title: Product Integration, EEMEA
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Product Integration, EEMEA What we’re hiring for We are hiring an Integration Manager to support customers across Eastern Europe, Middle East and Africa as part of our Ethoca EEMEA Product Integration team. This role is critical to how our customers experience Mastercard and Ethoca. You will work directly with customers integrating our solutions, many of whom are technically sophisticated, highly demanding, and expect strong guidance throughout the process. You will also partner closely with our Sales teams to support pre‑sales technical discussions and solution design. This is a hands‑on, customer‑facing role that requires technical fluency, structured execution, and the ability to drive clarity across complex stakeholder environments. About the role The role has two core responsibilities: Customer integration & delivery Act as the primary technical point of contact for customers during onboarding and post‑go‑live. Lead customers through end‑to‑end integrations, from kick‑off through configuration, testing, and launch. Support customers that require close engagement, frequent follow‑ups, and clear technical guidance. Review customer requirements and existing processes and recommend practical, scalable solutions. Build and maintain integration plans, track risks and mitigations, and provide clear status updates to customers and internal stakeholders. Own progress and outcomes — identifying issues early and driving resolution. Pre‑sales technical support Partner with Sales during pre‑sales engagements, acting as the technical expert in customer discussions. Support solution design, feasibility assessments, and technical Q&A during the sales cycle. Translate customer needs into actionable technical requirements for internal teams. Across both areas, you will work closely with internal stakeholders to get answers, unblock issues, manage expectations, and keep work moving. Who the ideal candidate is The ideal candidate is pragmatic, proactive, and comfortable owning complex integrations. You: Have experience implementing or supporting API‑ or SFTP‑based integrations, ideally in fintech, payments, or SaaS. Are comfortable working directly with customers on technical topics and managing demanding stakeholders. Can explain technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Have strong execution discipline — you build plans, track risks, manage dependencies, and follow through. Proactively engage internal teams to get answers and move work forward. Are solution‑minded and focused on outcomes, not theory. jobs.title: Midstream Facilities Engineer - Mature Assets
jobs.description: Are you excited to plan, organize, and implement continuous improvement methods? Would you like to take ownership of the strategy and execution of Carbon Out methods? The role is a key position within the diverse, multi-disciplined, results focused team. The successful candidate shall meet the technical requirements listed below whilst being focused on collaborating with internal and external resources to deliver on strategic goals. Partner with the best As a Midtsream Facilities Engineer – Mature Asset, you will be responsible for the maintenance activities or new installation of electrical and mechanical systems in Bari site of related facilities equipment. In this role, you will be responsible for: Defining high-level upstream and midstream concepts (wellsite, CPF, compression, treating, water handling, export). Identify processing, compression, power, water disposal, storage, and export limitations. Providing screening-level CAPEX/OPEX estimates and key cost drivers. Experience in utilizing QUESTOR a plus. Assessing gathering systems, pipelines, third-party access, tariffs, and specification constraints. Identifying facilities-related risks for the asset risk register (sour service, emissions, integrity, permitting, brownfield complexity). Reviewing facilities documentation, integrity records, and brownfield tie-in constraints. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: BSc in Chemical, Mechanical, or Process Engineering. Have 15+ years of experience in upstream and/or midstream facilities engineering. Strong background in concept selection, brownfield modifications, cost estimating, and process safety. Experience supporting asset evaluations and transactions. Possess excellent oral and written communication skills Demonstrate effective problem identification and solution skills on complex projects Show proven analytical and organizational ability Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. jobs.title: Site Architect
jobs.location: Ras Al Khaimah, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: A Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Education: Bachelor's degree in Architecture, Master's degree preferred. Experience: Minimum of 10 years of experience in architecture and construction management, with experience gained working on site and in a construction supervision capacity. Experience of working on large scale horizontal building projects is also desired

jobs.title: Inspector - Utilties (Rails/Tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Utilities Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect and monitor all civil utilities works on site to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and authority standards. Supervise installation activities related to utilities such as stormwater drainage, sewerage, potable water, irrigation, telecom, electrical duct banks, and utility corridors. Verify line and level, pipe bedding, backfilling, compaction, and trench works before proceeding with subsequent activities. Ensure materials delivered to site are approved and comply with project specifications and quality standards. Conduct inspections for excavation, pipe laying, manholes, chambers, thrust blocks, and utility crossings. Review and monitor contractor’s work progress in accordance with approved method statements and construction schedules. Coordinate with surveyors and contractors to ensure utilities are installed at correct alignment and elevations. Raise NCRs, site observations, and non-conformance reports for any deviations from specifications or approved drawings. Witness testing activities including hydrostatic tests, pressure tests, leakage tests, compaction tests, and CCTV inspections where applicable. Ensure proper implementation of approved shop drawings and IFC drawings during construction activities. Monitor health, safety, and environmental compliance during utility installation activities. Coordinate with utility authorities and consultants for inspections, approvals, and clearance requirements. Maintain daily inspection records, site diaries, and quality documentation related to utilities works. Verify reinstatement works for roads, pavements, and affected areas after utility installation. Attend progress meetings, technical discussions, and coordination meetings with contractors and stakeholders. Ensure all WIRs (Work Inspection Requests) are inspected and closed within the required timeframe. Review as-built drawings and verify completed utility works before handover. Monitor subcontractor performance and ensure workmanship meets project quality requirements. Support the Resident Engineer and Civil Engineer in resolving site technical issues and construction conflicts. Ensure utility works comply with relevant authority requirements. Prepare snag lists and verify closure of defects prior to project completion and handover Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent professional qualification (HND/Diploma in Civil Engineering acceptable) Minimum 7 years of professional experience in utilities, infrastructure, or rail projects Proven experience on UAE-based rail projects with knowledge of local construction standards and regulatory requirements Proficiency in reading, interpreting, and enforcing compliance with technical drawings, specifications, and contract documents Experience preparing and maintaining inspection logs, test reports, and site documentation in accordance with contract procedures Familiarity with Non-Conformance Reporting (NCR) and Site Observation Report (SOR) procedures Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) certification or demonstrated commitment to HSE best practices Proficiency with construction management software and digital record-keeping platforms Excellent communication and stakeholder coordination skills with proven ability to liaise with contractors, consultants, and utility authorities Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with ability to resolve technical issues independently Meticulous attention to detail with proven track record of maintaining accurate site records and ensuring specification compliance

jobs.title: Senior Engineer - QA/QC
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: Job Summary: The QA/QC Engineer is responsible for ensuring that all project activities, materials, and workmanship comply with applicable quality standards, specifications, and client requirements. The role involves monitoring, inspecting, and reporting on quality-related aspects throughout the project lifecycle to ensure successful project delivery.   Key Responsibilities: Develop, implement, and maintain the Project Quality Management Plan (QMP). Ensure compliance with project specifications, standards, and contractual requirements. Conduct inspections and tests on materials, equipment, and construction/installation activities. Review and approve Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs), method statements, and quality procedures. Coordinate with project teams, consultants, and contractors on all quality-related matters. Monitor non-conformance reports (NCRs) and ensure proper corrective and preventive actions are implemented. Prepare and maintain quality documentation, reports, and records. Perform internal audits and participate in external audits as required. Ensure proper calibration and control of measuring and testing equipment. Need experience in Marine Projects  Support continuous improvement initiatives related to quality performance. Qualifications & Experience: Minimum of ten (10) years of experience in QA/QC within similar projects. Proven experience in quality control and assurance practices in construction/engineering projects. Familiarity with international standards such as ISO 9001. Skills & Competencies: Strong knowledge of QA/QC procedures, standards, and documentation. Experience with inspection, testing, and auditing processes. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and coordination abilities. Proficiency in reporting and documentation tools

jobs.title: Manager - Bids
jobs.description: Job Summary Manages the end-to-end bid process for bids from identification to award. Oversees the process of obtaining quotes from sub-consultants as required and leads negotiations of appropriate terms and conditions based on AECOM's standard contract and Risk committee requirements. jobs.title: Planning Engineer (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: Ensure project schedules comply with RTA specifications, guidelines, and standard work breakdown structures (WBS) for roads, bridges, and infrastructure projects. ·         Coordinate planning activities in line with RTA approval procedures, including interim milestones, traffic staging, and sectional handover requirements. ·          Monitor and integrate RTA authority approvals (NOCs, permits, traffic diversion approvals, utility coordination) into the project schedule. • Review and assess contractors’ programs against RTA contractual milestones, key performance indicators (KPIs), and phasing requirements. ·         Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. ·         Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. ·         Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. ·         Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. ·         Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. ·         Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. ·         Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. ·         Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. ·         Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. ·         Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. ·         Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. ·         Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. ·         Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. ·         Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. ·         Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. ·         Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports. A minimum of 8+ years’ experience in the Planning / Project Controls field is required. Applicants would ideally have worked on major programs in transport. A Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or Construction Management. Advanced knowledge of ORACLE Primavera P6 Project Management Software. Ability to develop, monitor and control schedules. Hands on working knowledge of WBS coding structure, cost /schedule variance analysis, Earned Value Management (EVMS), project trend, contract change and claims. Working knowledge of Time Impact Analysis techniques.

jobs.title: Inspector - Tunnels
jobs.description: Role Summary Responsible for inspecting and monitoring rail tunnel construction activities to ensure full compliance with approved drawings, specifications, contract requirements, applicable railway standards, and health, safety, and environmental regulations. Key Responsibilities Conduct site inspections of rail tunnel works including excavation, segmental lining, waterproofing, secondary lining, track bed, and associated civil works. Monitor contractor activities to ensure compliance with approved method statements, construction sequences, and quality procedures. Verify materials, workmanship, equipment installation, and testing activities in accordance with project requirements and rail industry standards. Ensure compliance with railway-specific safety requirements, including safe access/egress, permits to work, and tunnel working procedures. Maintain detailed daily site records including labour, plant, progress, inspections, test results, and non-conformance reports. Review and report on construction progress, quality issues, and site non-conformities to the Resident Engineer (RE) and Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE). Assist in verifying tunnel alignment, levels, surveying checks, and as-built documentation. Coordinate with contractors, engineers, and client representatives during inspections, testing, and commissioning activities. Monitor and witness testing, commissioning, and handover activities, ensuring all required documentation is completed and closed out. Support the RE/ARE in resolving site issues and ensuring timely corrective and preventive actions. Ensure adherence to project HSE requirements and participate in safety inspections, audits, and toolbox talks. Experience 7–10 years’ experience in construction inspection on major rail infrastructure projects. Proven experience in tunnel works within rail, metro, or underground transit projects. Strong knowledge of tunnelling methods, segmental lining, rail standards, QA/QC procedures, and HSE requirements in live rail environments.         Diploma or Degree in Civil or Railway Engineering (or equivalent).

Coordinate with the Design Engineer to address client-requested modifications and ensure alignment with project objectives. Assist the Project Lead in reviewing and selecting materials and equipment in compliance with approved submittals. Support the Project Lead in drafting and issuing engineering correspondence. Identify and report non-conformities related to construction and engineering activities through the issuance of Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs). Attend progress and coordination meetings, assisting in resolving site issues such as drawing discrepancies and technical conflicts. Participate in snagging and project handover activities, ensuring all works meet quality and contractual standards. Provide expert technical support to the Resident Engineer (RE) to ensure smooth project execution. Liaise with the Design Engineer to implement necessary design modifications during construction. Submit daily site reports, highlighting key issues, progress, and observations to the Project Lead. Expedite client approvals to prevent delays and maintain project momentum.     Minimum of 15 years post-undergraduate experience MiddleEast and consultancy experience is required Bachelor's Degree in an Electrical Engineering field

jobs.title: Senior Managing Consultant, AML Subject Matter Expert, Advisors & Consulting Services
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Senior Managing Consultant, AML Subject Matter Expert, Advisors & Consulting Services Overview: The consulting services team at Mastercard is look for a senior candidate to deliver AML advisory services to our customers globally. The role, which is part of the consulting delivery team involves providing expert guidance to organizations on managing anti-money laundering (AML) risks, drive thought Leadership and in close collaboration with global product team, shape and support our Go To Market Strategy for AML Services. The candidate will have the opportunity to work with a high performing team across the organization (sales, global product, internal compliance teams) and become an integral part of success making the Mastercard ecosystem safer and more secure for our customers. The ideal candidate will be passionate about Financial Crime and have a strong background in AML, PEP & Sanctions. This role is key to the success of Mastercard in the AML ecosystem. Role Responsibilities involve a mix of strategic advice and hands-on implementation: • Risk Assessment: Conducting comprehensive, enterprise-wide AML and sanctions risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities and assess existing controls. • Policy and Procedure Development: Designing, reviewing, enhancing, and developing AML program governance, including tailored policies, procedures, and risk appetite statements aligned requirements • Compliance Monitoring and Testing: Developing and executing independent testing and audit procedures to evaluate the effectiveness of AML controls and identify compliance gaps. • Investigations and Due Diligence: Assisting with transaction monitoring, performing "lookbacks" (retrospective reviews), conducting customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD) for high-risk clients, and making recommendations on suspicious activity reporting (SARs). • Remediation and Regulatory Response: Advising clients on resolving deficiencies, managing remediation plans • System Implementation and Optimization: Assisting in the selection, implementation, tuning, and validation of AML technology systems and software to improve efficiency and accuracy. • Stakeholder Collaboration: Serving as a subject matter expert (SME), liaising with senior management, global product, legal, operations, and technology teams to implement effective compliance solutions. All About You • Years related business experience in AML advisory, product management, product development, business development in financial services and/or bankcard industry preferred. • Excellent oral and written communication, negotiation, project management and matrix-management skills are critical • Ability to interact and coordinate effectively with internal and external business partners • Ability to effectively direct and manage multiple functions • Ability to initiate and effectively lead change efforts • Business Acumen with the ability to provide solutions that drive positive results to the customer and Mastercard • Self-motivated with a proven track record of delivering success while operating within a team environment • Embody the MA leadership principles: demonstrate courage & resolution; encourage teamwork, integration, & inclusion; execute the global strategy; inspire & empower; operate with a sense of urgency Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organi …

jobs.title: Sales Manager
jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society. About the role The Sales Manager is responsible for providing technical expertise and support throughout the sales process, from initial customer engagement to post-sale implementation. This role involves analyzing customer requirements, preparing technical proposals, and delivering product demonstrations. The Sales Manager ensures that solutions are technically sound and aligned with customer needs. Responsible for developing and executing sales strategies driving order intake, profitability, and market share growth for industrial equipment and solutions. The role focuses on key accounts, EPCs, end users, and government-related industrial projects , while ensuring strong internal coordination with global factories, service centers, and regional business units. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales & Business Development Own and drive sales performance across GCC countries , UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, and Bahrain. Develop and execute country-level and regional sales plans aligned with business unit strategy . Lead the full sales cycle : opportunity identification, RFQ/tender review, commercial strategy, negotiation, and order booking. Build and maintain strong relationships with key end users, EPCs, consultants, and government entities . 2. Key Account & Stakeholder Management Act as the primary commercial contact for assigned strategic accounts across the GCC. Manage sensitive customer matters such as pricing discussions, contractual clarifications, delivery issues, and escalations in a professional and diplomatic manner. Ensure alignment with local agents and partners , while complying with company governance and agency policies. 3. Tendering & Commercial Governance Coordinate complex tender and proposal activities , ensuring close collaboration with global tendering teams, factories, and engineering. Ensure correct commercial positioning , margins, terms & conditions, and risk assessment for each opportunity. Ensure accurate order booking , including correct business unit ownership, SAP application codes, and P&L attribution. Support intercompany coordination where projects involve multiple legal entities or regions. 4. Internal Coordination & Execution Act as a bridge between customers and internal teams : engineering, project management, supply chain, factories, and service units. Follow up on order confirmations, manufacturing progress, logistics, and delivery milestones to ensure customer satisfaction. Drive internal alignment and proactively escalate risks that could impact delivery, cost, or customer relationship. 5. Sales Reporting & Market Intelligence Maintain accurate CRM pipeline, forecasts, and sales reports . Provide regular insights on market trends, competitor activities, and customer investment plans across the GCC. Support budgeting, forecasting, and strategic planning processes.. What we offer you As per local requirements Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Manager, SME Product & Partnership Management
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Manager, SME Product & Partnership Management Manager, SME Product & Partnership management role will support the Head of SME Product Development EEMEA, to lead the development & management of SME solutions and partnerships in EEMEA . These solutions and partnerships will support Issuers and acquirers as well as the end SME user. They may also support additional distribution partners like the governments, fintechs, and digital players. This cross-functional position will be interfacing with global product, regional sales teams, account management, business development, and external partners to ensure Mastercard maintains and grows its leadership in the SME segment. This role will work with the SME EEMEA Head, Global SME product team, Regional Commercialization Leads, regional delivery and account teams to develop Africa SME strategy, solutions and go to market plans to win more business and grow programs with our customers. Role: Partnership Strategy and Execution • Design and execute a strategic partnership roadmap to enhance differentiation (with fintechs, ISVs, platforms, ecosystem players, etc.) • Elevate partnerships beyond distribution to include co-creation and innovation, driving competitive advantage • Guide and manage partner onboarding and commercialization pipeline Product development • Support the segment and collaborate with regional management and specific issuers, acquirers & partners to develop customized programs for small businesses • Provide input into global SME product management activities • Complete opportunity assessments including business cases on new ideas and concepts. Product management • Support the tracking and delivery of portfolio results as well as the results of new product/product enhancements to determine effectiveness and refine strategy development. • Ensure product suite is continually updated to exceed cardholder and issuer expectations, industry standards, and competitive pressures. Support ownership of competitive assessments to feed into global product/product architect organization related to drive product development needs or bundled solutions • Manage performance of existing products and issuer implementations. • Continuously assess and optimize product economics to support issuance of right products for the right segments • Support Commercialization and Account teams with customer facing small business expertise and support related to small business portfolio business development with banks and non-traditional partners • Work with Commercialization and Account teams to develop solutions to support customer segment needs and differentiate product offering All About You • Solid experience in the payment/technology industry in product management. • Deep understanding of the SME ecosystem, including financial institutions, fintechs, ISVs, PayFacs, and platform players. • Understanding of card issuing and payments technologies. • Proven track record in bringing ideas from creation to execution. • Strong written and verbal communication skills, ability to present to customers and at conferences • Strong business acumen • Proven track record in GTM strategy and partnership development • Understanding of competitive offerings and industry trends in the markets. jobs.title: Senior Contracts Manager
jobs.description: Job Description Senior Contracts Manager The Senior Contracts Manager will be responsible to manage SP&S deals from a contracting and commercial perspective throughout the deal lifecycle, ensuring compliance with contract commitments and commercial risk assessment during all phases. In this role they negotiate contract terms and conditions, provide commercial and contractual leadership and direction throughout the entire deal life-cycle and develop practical and innovative ways to identify & mitigate contract risk. Partner the best The Senior Contracts Manager will be responsible for: Developing & negotiating detailed contractual agreements with customers, and leading cross-functional teams in the contract negotiation, contributing to define the overall deal strategy. Supporting the bid teams in proposal development, risk management (including deal risk and governance adherence - deal reviews, etc.), scope definition, pricing and incentives, customer communications and issue resolution. Managing all commercial and contractual related aspects of a project during the execution phase, including but not limited to commercial arrangements, Variation Orders, contract negotiation, amendments, adherence to contract requirements, etc. Ensuring compliance with the project specific contractual administration requirements (establish and maintain the project variation control system, managing the invoicing process, etc..). Providing advice, assistance and guidance to management, project managers, engineering, sourcing and other functions on project specific contractual matters. Ensuring commercial risks are identified and managed across the project scopes of work by controlling the overall project risk and opportunity review and reporting process. Managing negotiations in line with contract requirements, including supporting the formulation and negotiation of project contractual claims and counter claims. Fuel your passion Have a Bachelor’s Degree in Law/Business or related experience. Have detailed knowledge of energy contracts, terms and conditions and negotiation techniques and contracts negotiation. Strong commercial leadership skills with proven capability of working in a matrix environment and of leading by influence. Understanding of key contractual and commercial risks and areas for opportunity and ability to lead project risk review process and assessments. Negotiating Skills: To secure favorable outcomes, having the flexibility to understand and negotiate alternative contractual and commercial positions with customers (subject to approval). Strong commercial acumen and business understanding Excellent oral and written communication skills. Effective Team player. Be willing to travel. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. We work in hybrid model : 4 days from office and 1 day from home (remote) Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities · Comprehensive private medical care options · Safety net of life insurance and disability programs · Tailored financial programs · Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Sales Advisor - Proposals

jobs.title: Testing & Commissioning Engineer - Freight Facilities
jobs.description: Testing & Commissioning Engineer role will support Freight Facilities as part of a transformative rail infrastructure project. The scope includes formation preparation, track installation, freight yard development, and systems integration, ensuring seamless operations and world class standards. Candidates must have prior experience working on similar rail, freight facilities, or major infrastructure projects. Plan, coordinate, and supervise testing and commissioning activities for civil and infrastructure projects. Ensure all testing and commissioning works are carried out in accordance with project specifications, approved procedures, and authority requirements. Prepare and review testing and commissioning plans, procedures, checklists, and method statements. Coordinate with contractors, consultants, subcontractors, and authorities during testing and commissioning activities. Monitor testing activities for utilities networks including water supply, sewerage, stormwater, irrigation, and associated civil infrastructure. Witness and verify hydrostatic testing, pressure testing, leakage testing, gravity line testing, compaction testing, and other required tests. Ensure all materials, equipment, and installations are tested and commissioned as per approved standards and manufacturer recommendations. Review shop drawings, IFC drawings, specifications, and technical submittals before commencement of testing activities. Verify readiness of systems and completion of pre-commissioning requirements before final commissioning. Coordinate interface activities between civil, mechanical, electrical, and utility systems during commissioning stages. Ensure testing equipment and instruments are calibrated and valid prior to testing activities. Prepare and maintain testing records, commissioning reports, inspection reports, and handover documentation. Identify defects, non-conformities, and incomplete works during testing and ensure corrective actions are implemented. Raise NCRs and site observations for non-compliant works where required. Monitor contractor performance and ensure testing activities are completed within project schedules. Coordinate with utility authorities for inspections, approvals, and final connections. Attend testing, commissioning, and technical coordination meetings with project stakeholders. Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality requirements during all testing activities. Support project handover processes by ensuring all testing and commissioning documentation is complete and approved. Verify as-built drawings and O&M manuals before project closeout. Ensure compliance with requirements of authorities. Assist the Resident Engineer and Project Management team in resolving technical and commissioning-related issues. Monitor final snagging and ensure closure of all testing and commissioning comments before project handover.   Minimum Requirements:  The candidate would need to have prior experience in similar projects.  Middle East experience would be advantageous  Minimum of 5+ years of relevant site supervision experience  Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant Engineering Field + experience on Railway projects.

jobs.title: Engineer - Interface - Freight Facilities
jobs.description: We are seeking an Engineer - Interface to coordinate and control interfaces between civil works and multidisciplinary packages on a complex rail and infrastructure program. You will drive resolution of design and construction interfaces across structural, architectural, utilities, MEP, rail systems, roads, and wider infrastructure—ensuring safe, compliant, and constructible outcomes aligned to schedule and quality. We value discretion and professional conduct in operational environments. Key Responsibilities Coordinate and manage interfaces between civil works and Structural, Architectural, Utilities, MEP, Rail Systems, Roads, and Infrastructure disciplines. Review design drawings across packages; identify interface conflicts or gaps and drive timely resolution. Ensure smooth coordination between contractor, consultant, client, and subcontractors; establish clear communication pathways Monitor interface issues and maintain interface registers, trackers, and action logs with owners and due dates. Organize and lead interface/coordination meetings; issue minutes, decisions, and follow-up actions. Review RFIs, technical queries, and design changes related to interface matters; escalate risks and secure approvals as required. Ensure all interfacing works comply with project specifications, standards, and approved drawings. Coordinate construction sequencing, access requirements, and work windows between multiple contractors/packages. Identify potential clashes and assist in resolving technical conflicts before execution (design and construction phases). Support design coordination and constructability reviews, including temporary works and staging plans. Liaise with authorities and utility agencies to align on interface requirements, permits, and approvals. Track progress of interfacing activities and align dependencies with the integrated project schedule. Coordinate handover requirements and interface deliverables between contractors and across project phases. Assist in reviewing shop drawings, method statements, and material submissions for interface compliance. Maintain auditable records of interface communications, approvals, and action item closure. Ensure temporary works, diversions, and logistics do not adversely impact adjacent contractors or systems. Coordinate civil interfaces spanning Roads, Bridges, Tunnels, Stations, Drainage, Utilities, and Landscaping. Support risk identification, mitigation planning, and contingency for interface-related issues. Participate in site inspections to verify interface compliance during execution; close observations promptly. Ensure all stakeholders are informed of design revisions and construction changes impacting interfaces. Coordinate testing and commissioning interfaces between civil and other systems; verify pre-requisites and dependencies. Assist the Project Manager and Resident Engineer in resolving interface-related delays and conflicts. Monitor close-out and handover activities to confirm interface requirements are completed and accepted. Prepare interface progress reports and status updates for management and client review. • Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or a closely related engineering discipline. • Proven experience coordinating interfaces on large, multidisciplinary infrastructure programs (e.g., rail, roads, utilities). • Experience working on freight facilities

jobs.title: Senior Architect
jobs.description: Job Summary: A Senior Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Minimum 10 years demonstrable experience in the all aspects of supervision and project administration of the Architectural works related to large-scale projects within Middle East in the consulting industry. Bachelor's Degree in Architectural Engineering or equivalent. Membership of a recognized institution (i.e. PE/Peng, CEng Etc.) would be preferred. LEED GA or AP certifications would be a plus

jobs.title: Civil Inspector - (rails/ tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Civil Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive inspections of railway tracks and rail infrastructure at our freight facilities, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational excellence. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures, including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

jobs.description: We are seeking an experienced and creative Senior Urban Designer to lead the planning, design, and delivery of high-quality urban environments. The successful candidate will play a key role in shaping sustainable, inclusive, and commercially viable developments, working across multidisciplinary teams on complex urban projects. Responsibilities: Lead urban design projects from concept through to detailed design and implementation Develop innovative masterplans, public realm strategies, and urban frameworks Ensure design excellence aligned with sustainability, livability, and client objectives Provide design direction and mentorship to junior designers and wider project teams Translate strategic visions into practical, deliverable urban design proposals Contribute to feasibility studies, site analysis, and development strategies Prepare design guidelines, codes, and planning documentation Act as a key point of contact for clients, consultants, and government authorities Present design concepts and proposals to stakeholders and leadership teams Support business development activities, including proposals and pitches Work closely with architects, planners, engineers, landscape architects, and other disciplines Coordinate with internal teams to ensure consistency and quality across projects Contribute to integrated design solutions across mixed-use developments Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Urban Design, Architecture, Urban Planning, or a related field Typically 8+ years of relevant experience in urban design or masterplanning Proven track record of delivering large-scale, complex urban projects Strong portfolio demonstrating concept design, masterplanning, and built work Experience working in international or multidisciplinary environments is preferred

jobs.title: Senior Associate - Landscape
jobs.description: We are seeking a Senior Associate - Landscape to lead design, delivery and client engagement across our Middle East portfolio, advancing high-performing public realm and landscape projects from concept through to delivery. You will shape design outcomes, grow key client relationships, and mentor a multidisciplinary team to deliver resilient, context-driven solutions. Responsibilities: Lead landscape design across all stages (visioning, concept, schematic, detailed, IFC) with a strong focus on design excellence, constructability and value. Set design direction, run quality reviews, and ensure alignment with project briefs, budgets, and programs. Champion climate-appropriate, water-wise and resilient landscape strategies tailored to arid and semi-arid MEA conditions. Own scope, schedule, budget, risk, and change management across multiple concurrent workstreams. Coordinate seamlessly with urban design, architecture, civil, structures, MEP, environmental, and cost management teams. Oversee technical deliverables and ensure digital standards and workflows are followed. Lead design presentations and manage feedback to maintain scope, quality, and client satisfaction. Navigate regional authority processes to support approvals, permitting, and compliance requirements. Conduct regular project reviews to ensure alignment with client objectives and company standards. Prepare and deliver internal and external training sessions to enhance team skills and knowledge. Support the development and implementation of digital tools and workflows to improve efficiency and quality. Collaborate with regional teams to leverage expertise and resources for project success. Prepare and review technical reports, feasibility studies, and strategic frameworks. Bachelor’s or Master’s in Landscape Architecture 12–15+ years’ experience with a strong portfolio across complex, large-scale landscape projects. Proven leadership in design quality, interdisciplinary coordination, and project delivery. Demonstrable experience navigating regional authority requirements and approvals. Proficiency in  Revizto /Rhino/Grasshopper; familiarity with BIM/digital workflows.

jobs.title: Graduate - Civil Engineer إماراتيين (خلاصة القيد)
jobs.description: Graduates joining the business in a Graduate role and engaging in all elements of the ADVANCE program. The program is designed to introduce and build essential business and personal skills, helping to enhance the transition from student to professional. Graduates are assigned duties that will provide a broad, well-rounded learning experience within the field they are joining at Graduate entry level. The ADVANCE program lasts approx. 24 months but the successful completion of the program is only to be used as an indicator for strong performance in line with other indicators (strong or above performance rating etc.) As part of AECOM’s commitment to Emiratisation and in accordance with UAE labour law, only UAE Nationals with a family book will be considered for this role. Key Responsibilities Builds analytic and design skills. Conducts engineering design under the supervision of an experienced engineer. Creates designs, calculations, sketches, diagrams, schematic drawings, and final working drawings under the supervision of an experienced engineer. Collects and analyzes data under the supervision of an experienced engineer. Uses computer software as a tool for solving basic engineering problems. Performs work in accordance with agreed upon budget and schedule under supervision.   UAE National with a family book. Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering. Familiarity with engineering software such as AutoCAD, Civil 3D, or similar. Ability to work effectively in a team environment.

jobs.title: Engineer - Civil
jobs.description: We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Civil Engineer to join our dynamic team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. As a key member of our engineering department, you will play a crucial role in designing and overseeing complex civil engineering projects across the region. The Civil Engineer will support the Resident Engineer in supervising and managing the construction of stormwater drainage networks, micro tunneling works, and associated civil structures. The role ensures that all activities are executed in full compliance with contract requirements, approved drawings, specifications, and RTA/DM standards, maintaining strict adherence to quality, safety, and schedule objectives. Develop and implement QA/QC procedures applicable to stormwater and microtunneling works, including inspection and test plans (ITPs) and method statements. Conduct and follow up on inspections for installations, ensuring compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and quality standards. Monitor and review inspectors’ performance, ensuring effective supervision and documentation of site activities. Update and report progress for all assigned works to the Resident Engineer / Project Manager for inclusion in project progress reports. Bachelor's degree (BEng) in Civil Engineering; advanced degree preferred Professional Engineer (PE) certification or equivalent; willingness to obtain UAE certification if not already held Minimum of 15 years of experience in civil engineering, with a focus on infrastructure projects, specifically micro-tunneling project. Proven track record of successfully managing large-scale engineering projects Strong technical competency in structural design, geotechnical engineering, and construction methodologies Extensive background in  infrastructure works  , including roads, NDRC, utilities, drainage, and municipal services. Strong working knowledge of  DM regulations, approval workflows, and authority portals
