Coordinating with the team on site to ensure that contractual obligations are met. Perform Architectural inspections and material inspections. Issue NCRs to contractors and assist in taking corrective actions. Ensure that all inspection reports are accurate and in compliance with the requirements. Ensure that all execution and inspection are carried out as per inspection plan/drawings/codes and specifications. Ensure compliance of architectural works to design requirements, specifications and project plan. Preparing daily logs and reports. Maintain records of all submittals and testing inspections made, tests performed, work executed, etc. Performing all work in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Assisting with the inspection of completed works. Perform all other duties given by direct manager. Monitoring and reporting on HSE related matters and participating in HSE training and meetings   Bachelor Degree or Diploma in Architecture or relevant degree Minimum 5 to 7 years of experience in building construction

jobs.title: Document Controller
jobs.description: Maintain and control all project documents, drawings, and correspondence in accordance with company procedures. Ensure proper document numbering, filing, tracking, and distribution systems are maintained. Receive, register, and distribute incoming and outgoing documents. Maintain updated logs for: Drawings RFIs Submittals NCRs Material approvals Technical submissions Correspondence Ensure only latest approved revisions of drawings and documents are available at site. Coordinate with engineers, consultants, contractors, and project teams for document submissions and approvals. Upload and manage documents through client systems and EDMS platforms. Track pending submissions and follow up for approvals and responses. Maintain hard copy and electronic filing systems for easy retrieval of records. Prepare transmittals for document submissions and distribution. Archive superseded and obsolete documents according to project procedures. Ensure confidentiality and security of project records and company documents. Assist project team in preparation of reports, presentations, and project documentation. Support QA/QC team in maintaining quality records and inspection documents. Maintain accurate records of shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals. Coordinate document flow between site office, head office, consultant, and client. Prepare weekly and monthly document status reports. Ensure compliance with project document control procedures and ISO standards. Monitor deadlines for submissions and maintain document turnaround tracking. Assist during audits by providing required documentation and records. Maintain attendance records, staff documents, and administrative files if required. Ensure all handover documents and close-out records are properly compiled and archived. Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant field Minimum 7+ years of experience as a Document Controller on UAE infrastructure projects (DM, RTA, or similar) Strong knowledge of document control processes , including drawings, RFIs, submittals, NCRs, and material approvals Experience managing EDMS/Aconex , document tracking, transmittals, and revision control Ability to ensure latest approved documents are maintained and properly distributed Proven coordination skills with engineers, consultants, and contractors Experience supporting QA/QC documentation and maintaining project records Proficient in MS Office with strong organizational and communication skills

jobs.title: Engineer - Civil (Rail, Track)
jobs.description: Supervise and monitor railway track construction activities in compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and project requirements. Coordinate track works including: Ballasted track Ballastless/slab track Rail installation Sleeper installation Turnouts and crossings Track alignment and geometry Review and verify shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals related to track works. Ensure track construction complies with railway standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. Monitor earthworks, formation preparation, and subgrade conditions before track installation. Coordinate with survey teams for track alignment, levels, and setting out verification. Supervise rail welding, fastening systems, and track fixing activities. Inspect ballast placement, compaction, and track stabilization works. Verify rail levels, gauge, cant, alignment, and track geometry tolerances. Coordinate interface works with: Civil structures Stations Utilities Signaling systems MEP systems Ensure compliance with quality assurance and quality control procedures. Review inspection requests and ensure timely closure of WIRs and inspection records. Raise NCRs and site observations for non-compliant works. Monitor contractor progress against approved baseline program and construction schedule. Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports. Coordinate with client, consultant, contractor, and railway authorities during execution. Ensure implementation of approved safety procedures and railway operational safety requirements. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track systems. Participate in track inspections, snagging, and handover activities. Review as-built drawings and final documentation before project handover. Monitor material delivery, storage, and handling of rails, sleepers, fasteners, and track components. Assist Resident Engineer and Project Manager in resolving technical and construction issues. Ensure compliance with international railway standards and project specifications. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from a recognized institution Minimum 8 years of professional experience in civil engineering, with a strong focus on rail/metro track works and infrastructure projects Proven experience in monitoring formation preparation, subgrade conditions, and readiness for track installation Demonstrated expertise in coordinating with survey teams for track alignment, levels, and setting-out verification Hands-on experience supervising rail welding, fastening systems, and track installation/fixing activities Strong knowledge of ballast placement, compaction techniques, and track stabilization processes In-depth understanding of track geometry requirements , including rail levels, gauge, cant, alignment, and tolerance control Experience in coordinating complex interface works across multiple disciplines , including: Civil structures Stations Utilities Signaling systems MEP systems Ability to interpret and apply technical drawings, specifications, and inspection/test plans (ITPs) Strong communication, coordination, and problem-solving skills within a multi-disciplinary rail project environment

jobs.title: Inspector - Civil (Rails, Tracks)
jobs.description: Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects , particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures , including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

jobs.title: Engineer - Civil (Rail, Track)
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Civil Engineer specializing in freight infrastructure to join our team in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.  You will work collaboratively with project teams to ensure all projects meet regulatory standards, safety requirements, and operational efficiency goals while maintaining organized project documentation and timely delivery. Supervise and monitor construction in line with approved IFC drawings, specifications, method statements, and project requirements. Review and verify shop drawings, method statements, ITPs, and material submittals for track works and related systems. Monitor contractor progress versus the approved baseline schedule; highlight risks, slippage, and recovery plans. Prepare accurate daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports with metrics, photos, and look‑ahead. Coordinate with the client, consultant, contractor, and railway authorities during execution and inspections. Ensure implementation of approved HSE procedures and railway operational safety requirements. Support testing and commissioning for track systems; participate in inspections, snagging, and handover. Assist the Resident Engineer and Project Manager in resolving technical and construction issues. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or related discipline; postgraduate or rail-specific qualifications are an advantage. 10+ years of construction and supervision experience on major rail or freight projects Proficient in coordinating multidisciplinary interfaces (civil, stations, utilities, signaling, MEP) and dealing with rail authorities. Skilled in progress tracking, risk identification, and recovery planning against an approved baseline schedule. GCC/UAE experience is a must.

jobs.title: Inspector - Materials
jobs.description: We are hiring a materials Inspector in Ruwais for a freight project, You will be responsible for inspections, materials verification, documentation, and quality control. Duties Inspect site conditions prior to the start of construction activities to verify the compatibility of the site with the approved design and permanent works. Check for obstructions and document existing site conditions through photographs to prevent potential damage to existing utilities, structures, and landscaping. Review and verify materials delivered to the site for conformance with approved samples, specifications, and equipment lists; report any non-conformances to the Resident Engineer (RE). Ensure that all materials are properly stored and protected at the site to maintain quality and prevent damage or contamination. Inspect construction of both temporary and permanent works to confirm compliance with contract documents, approved shop drawings, and project specifications. Prepare and maintain accurate daily inspection logs, test reports, and site records in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Monitor site safety practices and ensure that all works are carried out following approved safety procedures; report unsafe practices or incidents to the RE immediately. Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for any deviation from approved materials, specifications, or procedures, and follow up on corrective actions. Assist in the inspection of completed works, prepare punch lists, and monitor testing, commissioning, and handover activities to ensure quality compliance. Review and provide recommendations on Contractors’ Requests for Information (RFIs) related to civil materials and testing requirements. Evaluate requests for material variances or substitutions and provide technical comments and recommendations to the RE for approval. Read and interpret working drawings and related specifications, enforce compliance on-site, and prepare inspection and material testing reports. Coordinate with third-party testing laboratories and ensure all material tests are performed as per project specifications and standards. Ensure that all material approvals, test results, NCRs, and inspection records are properly documented and maintained for monthly reporting and project close-out. Coordinate with relevant authorities for material approvals, testing, and compliance verification. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or related field. Minimum of 7+ years of professional experience  working as a Material Inspector, with a strong focus on large-scale infrastructure projects. Candidates must have proven experience in: Rail and Metro projects  (such as UAE Metro, Etihad Rail, or similar international rail systems) Freight yard, rail or track infrastructure projects , including track works, ballast installation, and rail system components Strong knowledge of international and regional standards for civil materials and testing (e.g., ASTM, AASHTO, BS/EN) and QA/QC processes (ITPs, WIRs, NCRs, SORs). Proven experience reviewing shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and managing third‑party testing labs. Familiarity with authority processes in the UAE/GCC Excellent documentation, reporting, and stakeholder coordination skills; proficiency with common CDE/reporting tools is an advantage.

jobs.title: Graduate - Cost Management Surveyor
jobs.description: Graduates joining the business in a Graduate role and engaging in all elements of the ADVANCE program.  The program is designed to introduce and build essential business and personal skills, helping to enhance the transition from student to professional. Graduates are assigned duties that will provide a broad, well-rounded learning experience within the field they are joining at Graduate entry level. The ADVANCE program lasts approx. 24 months but the successful completion of the program is only to be used as an indicator for strong performance in line with other indicators. Role Overview Are you ready to launch your career in project surveying and commercial activities with a global leader in infrastructure and built asset advisory services? AECOM's Project Cost Consultancy (PCC) team is seeking a motivated  Graduate Project Surveyor  to join our dynamic and multi-disciplinary  Infrastructure team . As a Graduate Project Surveyor, you will play a key role in supporting all commercial activities throughout the pre and post contract phases of construction projects. Key Responsibilities Assisting Quantity Surveying staff in the  Infrastructure Team  on both pre-contract and post-contract deliverables. Undertakes responsibilities under the supervision of a more senior Project Surveyor Completes the measurement of quantities for input into cost plans, cost estimates and bills of quantities Assists with the preparation of tender documentation Assists with viability studies and cost estimates Assists with implementing, maintaining and monitoring cost control procedures throughout the duration of a project Assists with providing necessary input related to financial claims Assists with evaluating variations, additions and omissions, etc, and incorporating these into monthly cost reports     Assists with preparing, pricing and negotiating change orders with clients and contractors Market tests rates and analyses tender returns for input into rates databases and estimates Assists with upkeep of the technical library, including cost data, benchmarks and maintenance of the various asset databases used for life cycle cost analysis and operational cost estimates.           Assists with report formatting, data management and quality assurance procedures across all core service lines.                                                           Undertakes primary research according to project requirements under the supervision.   Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an accredited University Candidates must be recent graduates, having graduated within the last 24 months or no earlier than September 2024, to be eligible for AECOM's Graduate Program. Successful applicants must have achieved a minimum GPA of 3.2 and above. If you hold a Master’s Degree, it should have started within 12 months of completing your Bachelor’s Degree to ensure continuous education and meet program requirements. Candidates with more than 1 year of postgraduate work experience are not eligible for the program and are encouraged to apply for our professional-level roles instead.

jobs.title: Forward Deployment Engineer
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

The Mission
We believe the transformative potential of AI is significantly under-realised in our region. To bridge this gap, we are incubating a new division within Careem dedicated to Enterprise AI Transformation.
We are reimagining and building the future of work to simplify and improve how businesses function in the era of GenAI with WorkOS: a next-generation ‘operating system’ designed to deploy, orchestrate, and improve AI agents across every customer and operational journey (and increasingly autonomously). This isn’t just automation; it’s about creating a hybrid workforce where self-learning AI agents handle the heavy lifting, empowering our people to focus on the highest-value, most creative work.

The Team
This group operates as a startup within Careem with a distinct mandate: move fast, build boldly, and deliver production-grade AI systems that fundamentally change how the region leverages technology.

The Opportunity
We are looking for an exceptional founding Backend Engineer to build the WorkOS platform. This is a role for a systems-thinker who wants to work at the intersection of high-scale distributed systems and AI orchestration. You will be responsible for building the "brain" of WorkOS, the engine that deploys and manages autonomous agents across the region’s most complex operational journeys at massive scale.

As one of the first engineers in WorkOS, you will have a disproportionate impact on our technical foundation, culture, and the speed at which we move from 0 to 1.

What You’ll Do

  • Architect and Build the WorkOS Engine: Design and build the scalable backend services and architectures required to orchestrate complex AI workflows and real-time data streaming. Leverage novel technologies to build cutting-edge capabilities.
  • Set the Engineering Gold Standard: As a founding member, you will establish our backend best practices, from CI/CD and observability to security and system integrity, ensuring a high-velocity but rigorous engineering culture.
  • Solve for Distributed Complexity: Navigate the unique challenges of integrating AI agents into legacy enterprise stacks, ensuring low-latency communication and robust data consistency.
  • Database design: Build our data infrastructure, leveraging relational, schemaless, and vect …

jobs.title: Founding Forward Deployed Engineer - WorkOS - Enterprise Agentic AI
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

The mission
We believe the transformative potential of AI is significantly under-realised in our region. To bridge this gap, we are incubating a new division within Careem dedicated to Enterprise AI Transformation.

We are reimagining and building the future of work to simplify and improve how businesses function in the era of GenAI with WorkOS: a next-generation ‘operating system’ designed to deploy, orchestrate, and improve AI agents across every customer and operational journey (and increasingly autonomously). This isn’t just automation; it’s about creating a hybrid workforce where self-learning AI agents handle the heavy lifting, empowering our people to focus on the highest-value, most creative work.

The team
This group operates as a startup within Careem with a distinct mandate: move fast, build boldly, and deliver production-grade AI systems that fundamentally change how the region leverages technology.

The opportunity

We are looking for a Founding Forward Deployed Engineer - WorkOS - Enterprise Agentic AI to be the vanguard of the WorkOS mission. This is a role for a "heretic": someone who cares more about solving the problem and delivering value than following a rigid spec. You will be the bridge between our core AI engine and the messy, high-stakes reality of our customers’ operations.
As an FDE, you aren't sitting in the back office; you are on the front lines, embedded with internal Careem verticals and external enterprise partners. In partnership with your implementation squad, consisting also of business transformation consultants, you will identify their deepest friction points and build the "special ops" code that proves the power of WorkOS. Your work is the proof of concept that will redefine how the region works.

What You’ll Do
  • Tip of the Spear: Lead the deployment of WorkOS in the field. You will be responsible for understanding a customer's operational "chaos" and architecting an AI-driven solution that fixes it in weeks, not months.

jobs.title: Engineer - Planning
jobs.description: Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports.

jobs.title: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor
jobs.location: AE-ABU DHABI-AL GHAITH YARD, MUSSAFAH INDUSTRIAL 16th STREET
jobs.description: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor Are you a Mechanical Field Service Engineer looking for an interesting and inspiring opportunity? Would you like to be part of our Turbomachinery Process Solutions Product Line? Join our Industrial and Energy Technology Team! Our Industrial and Energy Technology (IET) operates across 120 countries and serves the entire energy value chain and industrial sector. We hold an extensive portfolio of rotating equipment, process flow, transmission technology and lifecycle services. Our Engineering Transformation teamwork to improve the quality of our Engineering processes. Partner with the best As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressors, you will be coordinating and overseeing work at the field site, providing quality service to the customer. In addition, your Job role may include providing the planning necessary for the job including instructions to the crew and equipment. As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor, you will be responsible for Providing technical advice and support to clients and craft-labour in the installation, commissioning and maintenance of Baker Hughes-supplied Centrifugal Compressors. Unloading and Inspection of the Centrifugal Compressor & its major auxiliaries at the installation site and their placement on the foundation. Improving Geometric dimensional check of foundation, equipment and auxiliary skids positioning, levelling, and alignment Performing Installation, commissioning & Startup of various types of Centrifugal Compressors MCL, BCL, PCL, and SRL supplied by Baker Hughes at customer sites. Performing minor & major inspections of Centrifugal Compressors. Involving assembly, disassembly, checking and adjusting clearances evaluating wear and damage state of Couplings, lubricated-tooth, and dry-elastic types, thrust bearings and journal bearings of various kinds, gas seals, labyrinth and honeycomb types, Oil seals, gauged floating rings types, Dry gas seals or oil mechanical seals, Labyrinth or honeycomb inner seals, Diaphragms or diaphragms bundle and volutes. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a bachelor’s in engineering with 5+ years of relevant industry experience. Have working experience in Rotating equipment in the Field at Customer Sites. Have excellent Functional/Technical, Customer Focus, Communication, Interpersonal, and Team working skills. Have prior experience in Installation and/or Maintenance activities of Centrifugal Compressors in the Field at Customer Sites Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: When assignments are complete, in general, you won't be required to visit the office, however, you can operate from Home and perform basic tasks like completing previous assignment reports, mobilization-related aspects, etc before you take on your next projects. The company shall ensure a work-life balance for the individual. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. jobs.title: Field Engineer Supervisor - Wireline
jobs.description: Field Supervisor – Wireline Would you like the opportunity to learn cutting-edge technology? Do you enjoy being part of a successful team? Join our Wireline Team! We are the leader in well construction and production. Our innovative physical and digital solutions improve efficiency, production and maximize reservoir value. Partner with the best Baker Hughes is seeking an experienced and dynamic Field Supervisor for our Wireline services, specifically focusing on open hole logging operations. The ideal candidate will have significant hands-on experience in wireline operations, with a preference for those who have worked with Baker Hughes products. In this role, you will lead a team of field operators, ensuring the safe and efficient execution of wireline services while maintaining the highest standards of quality and customer satisfaction. As a Field Supervisor - Wireline , you will be responsible for: Under broad direction leads crews and equipment at the workshop and wellsite in the execution of wireline logging and completions services Minor maintenance of equipment and assisting in actuating the completions equipment. Working with the Customer Representative and drill crew on location to coordinate operations at the rig. Providing a record of all assemblies as run at the well site to the Customer Representative and promotes and maintains good customer relations. Being primary contact to work with the Customer Representative advising them on the current challenges at the wellsite exerting significant latitude in determining how to best troubleshoot the well. Applying Health, Safety and Environment (HS&E) standards and procedures in all situations to ensure compliance is maintained. Leading and developing a project resources team to successfully execute strategies for the management of customer projects. Showing flexibility and be adaptable to changes to accommodate operations timing, travel requirements and hitch duration. ​ Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a minimum of 7 years of experience in wireline operations, with a strong focus on open hole logging. Proven experience with Baker Hughes wireline products and technologies is a must. Be happy to travel to international locations to work with customers on site. Have experience in prior field-based roles. Show the ability to adapt and change in a fast-moving, dynamic environment. Have an excellent awareness of Health, Safety and Environmental compliance and potential risks. Have excellent leadership skills and the ability to manage and motivate a diverse team in a dynamic work environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: FIELD SUPV I - WLE

jobs.title: Oil & Gas Sales Specialist - UAE
jobs.description: Bring your energy to Aggreko as Oil & Gas Sales Specialist based in Abu Dhabi. You will have a sales plan (account portfolio) where you will manage and deliver growth within your current customers , target and win back your lapsed customers , and prospect target customers (new clients) with a focus on growth coupled with profitability. First class people skills are essential as well as a tenacity to succeed and be professional in all that you do. The role is 40%/60% split between new business prospecting and existing account growth. What you’ll do as Oil & Gas Sales Specialist - Abu Dhabi: Responsible for targeting, developing & acquiring customers within your sales plan. Motivated to meet and exceed targets. Revenue targets and sales KPI driven. Ensure 3rd party costs are appropriately priced. (Fuel, Freight & Rehire) Establishes and maintain effective working relationships with customers and colleagues. Process driven, following Aggreko’s sales process (sector sales excellence), as well as all CRM and CPQ processes. (salesforce) Minimum sales activity requirements and CRM & CPQ compliance requirements. Develop and execute a sales and marketing plan for developing business, including product / sector revenue targets. Identify new customers / business with a view to growing key account portfolio. To ensure that the network of current and potential customers is expanded and maintained through weekly external face to face and telephone sales calls. Prepare and submit proposals that include technical and commercial details of the client’s needs in accordance with Aggreko’s terms and conditions. To acquire a sound knowledge of internal administrative practice, including all hire procedures and the quick track system, product knowledge and the operation of the depot overall. You are: Experience within the temporary power & HVAC / Oil & Gas industry Degree qualification in sales or in a technical discipline OR Minimum 5 years of successful high-level sales experience in the industrial services environment. Dedication and commitment to achieving objectives. Experience of selling (or renting) capital equipment or selling a technical / engineering solutions or services. ( Solution Selling ) Proven track record in delivering results in line with targets set. Oil & Gas experience in the UAE is a plus. Here’s what you’ll get: Competitive compensation package Industry-leading benefit plans including medical insurance Continuous training and development with career growth opportunities Safety-focused culture Our people are can-do, positive, resilient and persistent. If that feels like you, apply now and build your career with the people bringing energy to the world. #LI-MK1 Equal employment opportunity We welcome people from different backgrounds and cultures, and respect people’s unique skills, attitudes and experiences. We encourage everyone to be themselves at work because we know that’s how we do our best, for each other, for our customers, for the communities where we work, and for our careers. We are an equal opportunity employer. If you apply for a role at Aggreko, we will consider your application based on your qualifications and experience, and not on your race, colour, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

jobs.title: Material Inspector (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: • Inspect and verify all construction materials delivered to site to ensure compliance with RTA specifications, approved material submittals, and contract requirements . • Check material source approvals, delivery notes, batch certificates, and test reports prior to acceptance and use at site. • Carry out sampling and witnessing of laboratory and field tests for soil, aggregates, concrete, asphalt, steel, and other civil materials as per approved ITPs. • Ensure materials used for roadworks, bridges, culverts, drainage, and utility works conform to approved specifications and drawings. • Monitor storage, handling, and protection of materials to prevent contamination, segregation, or damage. • Verify compliance with RTA-approved suppliers, manufacturers, and testing laboratories . • Coordinate with the Material Engineer and Site Engineer regarding testing schedules, approvals, and material-related issues. • Inspect asphalt paving, concrete works, earthworks, and backfilling to ensure correct materials and workmanship are used. • Review and submit Work Inspection Requests (WIRs) related to materials and civil works. • Maintain accurate inspection records, test results, and material logs for QA/QC and as-built documentation. • Identify non-conforming materials, raise NCRs and site observations , and follow up on corrective actions. • Ensure compliance with HSE requirements , especially during material handling, sampling, and testing activities. • Monitor material usage and quantities to avoid wastage, misuse, or deviation from approved specifications . • Assist in RTA inspections and audits , providing required documentation and site support. • Report daily site activities, material inspections, and testing status to the Material Engineer / Resident Engineer . Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field RTA/ DCL  approval is preferred 7+ years of relevant experience in laboratory materials testing including field inspection of samples and materials. Middle East experience preferred Relevant experience in comparable roads and bridges projects
